text: Multi-view feature selection aims to enhance the modeling capability of machine learning models for complex problems by integrating information from multiple views and selecting the most crucial and representative features. Current multi-view feature selection methods focus on the distribution and information differences brought by different views, while overlooking the issue of noise in multi-view data. In this paper, we propose a noise-tolerant multi-view feature selection algorithm. Specifically, we integrate view weights, sample weights, and feature weights into a unified framework to construct the objective function for multi-view feature selection. Subsequently, we employ a novel alternating iterative optimization algorithm to solve the proposed objective function and perform multi-view feature selection. During this process, noise samples are assigned smaller weights, enhancing the algorithm’s noise tolerance. Finally, a series of experiments are conducted on classification and regression tasks. The proposed algorithm achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, particularly on datasets containing noise. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag London Ltd., part of Springer Nature 2026.
label: human

text: With their inherent capacity for massive connectivity, ultra-low latency and high reliability, B5G networks provide an ideal infrastructure to support the diverse and dynamic requirements of IoT (Internet of Things) communication. Originally designed to initiate, modify and terminate multimedia sessions over IP networks, the Session Initiation Protocol (SIP), a standardized protocol developed by the 3GPP, has emerged as a promising protocol for enabling communication and coordination in IoT environments. Nonetheless, SIP encounters a multitude of Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) threats, with INVITE flooding attacks emerging as a notable challenge. Traditional IoT-IDS (Intrusion Detection System) relies on machine learning models trained on local data of their deployment context. However, such a model may not detect some attack patterns observed in other deployment contexts. We propose a design approach based on federated learning, and in which different IDS collaborate to achieve early detection of any INVITE flooding attack faced by any of them. The results show the effectiveness of the framework in detecting and mitigating INVITE flooding attacks across a various of flow intensities under realistic SIP operational conditions. Performance evaluations under different relevant scenarios demonstrate the robustness of the proposed framework. Experiments show that federated learning (FL) enhances the analysis of SIP flooding attacks, improving accuracy from 47 to 99% through knowledge sharing. The FL model with a GRU architecture and a FedAvgM aggregation function delivers the best performance, even in varied scenarios. © The Author(s) 2026.

text: Breast cancer survival prediction plays a significant role in better understanding the relationship between clinical features and demographic features to improve decision-making among clinicians. This is done with the help of machine learning models, which assist clinicians in making decisions for the improvement of patients. However, there has been an over-saturation of machine learning models in breast cancer survival prediction, of which the only distinction is the difference in data sampling used and better tuning parameters. This study explores the possibilities of a recent deep learning model called Transformers as a breast cancer survival prediction model. The breast cancer dataset used for the study ranges from 2006 until 2015. The dataset was retrieved from the University Malaya Medical Centre (UMMC), Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. The dataset contains 26 variables comprising 24 categorical variables and two numerical variables. A comparison of performance matrices on Transformer models such as BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers), RoBERTa (Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach) and ALBERT (A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations) to existing machine learning models such as random forest (RF), decision tree (DT), support vector machine (SVM), logistic regression (LR), k-nearest neighbours (KNN), artificial neural network (ANN) and extreme gradient boosting (XGB) was conducted. In terms of overall performance, the Transformer models produced scores that were close to those of other machine learning models. The best accuracy among the Transformer models was the ALBERT model at (81.1%) while the best accuracy among the machine learning models was the LR model at (83.4%). Based on the Transformer models’ close outcomes compared to machine learning models, Transformers can be introduced as an alternative prediction model in breast cancer survival studies. Clinical Trial Number: Not Applicable © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: Sentiment analysis plays a crucial role in understanding public opinion across Arabic social media platforms; however, challenges such as morphological richness, dialectal diversity, and contextual ambiguity hinder model performance. This study evaluates the impact of annotation quality and model architecture on the accuracy of Arabic sentiment analysis by comparing traditional machine learning algorithms with transformer-based architectures. A novel dataset was developed using an active annotation strategy that combines human expertise and artificial intelligence (AI) assisted labelling through Chat Generative Pre-Trained Transformer versions 3.5 and 4. This study investigates how annotation quality affects classification performance metrics —accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score— across various classifiers. The experimental results reveal that annotation consistency exerts a stronger influence on performance than model complexity. Classical machine learning models such as k nearest neighbours, multinomial naïve Bayes and random forest performed best with ChatGPT 4-labelled data, reflecting the lexical coherence of AI generated annotations. Conversely, context-sensitive models including support vector machine and logistic regression, achieved superior results with human-labelled datasets, emphasizing the value of human semantic interpretation. Transformer-based architectures—AraBERTv2, CAMeL Lab BERT, and XLM-RoBERTa—outperformed all other models, demonstrating robust contextual understanding and resilience to annotation noise. The findings indicate that integrating AI-assisted annotation with transformer-based deep learning provides a scalable and linguistically adaptive framework for Arabic sentiment analysis. This study advances both theoretical understanding and practical applications by proposing a balanced human–artificial intelligence annotation paradigm that enhances the efficiency and interpretability of Arabic sentiment analysis models. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: The 6G networks have made the need to ensure privacy of the user data as well as high-performance communication urgent. The centralized machine learning models possess several issues such as high latency, privacy breach and non-scaled performance especially applied on dynamic distributed systems such as Human Activity Recognition (HAR). To address these shortcomings, the paper suggests a new framework would integrate Federated Learning (FL) with Edge Intelligence (EI) and Differential Privacy (DP). The proposed model allows decentralized activation of activity recognition models in edge devices themselves, thereby eliminating the need to transfer raw data to the central servers. Differential Privacy boasts an extra level of privacy since it adds a controlled noise to the gradient updates hence protection against data reconstruction attacks. The model was tested on the UCI-HAR data with 5-fold cross-validation and achieved impressive. It had an average accuracy of 99.25, an F1-score of 0.9871, and a Kappa score of 0.9909, which is a strong predictive performance and consistency. Also, the False Negative Rate (FNR) and False Positive Rate (FPR) decreased to 0.0027 and 0.0115, respectively, which indicates the accuracy of the model. Unlike other baseline models like SVM and CNN-BiLSTM, the given system significantly reduces the latency and communication overhead at a high accuracy and privacy guarantees. It can thus be used well in real-time smart cities, healthcare, and industrial IoT. In general, this research advances safe and effective edge-based AI schemes towards 6G systems. Federated Learning, Edge Intelligence, and Differentiated privacy combined to create secure, decentralized activity recognition is an innovation. It is a very accurate method that keeps the user privacy, reduces latency and lets the real-time processing operate without showing raw data beyond the 6G network. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: Developing reliable models for detecting fraud is increasingly vital amidst the digitalization of our financial institutions and activities. While graph-based fraud detection models showed promising performances in recent years, the underpinning fraud definition and evaluation process they utilized rely heavily on a limited set of labeled datasets. This raises concerns about their vulnerability to distributional shifts and adversarial strategies often exhibited by real-life fraudsters. In response, we propose a novel scenario for graph fraud detection called Multi-round Adversarial Fraud Detection. Here, the fraud detection model is trained and evaluated iteratively on an adversarially evolving graph. This scenario more closely mimics fraud detection activities in real life while being less reliant on the underlying datasets since the graph evolution is induced by generator functions rooted in common fraud behavior. We show that existing models struggle to achieve good multi-round performance under the proposed scenario with F1 scores that consistently hover below 56 percent on subsequent rounds. To improve the aforementioned performance we propose Temporally Pre-trained Node Embedder (TPNE), a module that leverages self-supervised pre-training approach, explicitly separating and enhancing temporal information across multiple rounds. TPNE is both model and label-agnostic, improving the best-performing baseline by up to 4.6 percent in on-round F1 score and up to 32.9 percent in final recall. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: Purpose: Accurate prediction of postoperative pain can improve recovery quality and guide personalized pain management. This study aimed to develop and validate a machine learning (ML) model that predicts the presence of postoperative pain using biosignals recorded in the post-anesthesia care unit (PACU). Methods: Adult patients who underwent surgery between January 2021 and December 2022 at Chungnam National University Hospital (CNUH) (n = 21,855) and Chungnam National University Sejong Hospital (CNUSH) (n = 2,356) in South Korea were included. Electrocardiography (ECG) and photoplethysmography (PPG) signals were continuously recorded during the postoperative period in the PACU. From these signals, heart rate variability (HRV) and surgical pleth index (SPI) features were extracted. These biosignal features, together with demographic variables, were used as input features for training the ML models, including logistic regression, support vector machine, multilayer perceptron, random forest, and XGBoost. The model was developed and internally validated using the CNUH dataset, while external validation was performed using the independent CNUSH dataset to assess generalizability. Results: In the test dataset of 1068 patients, 961 reported postoperative pain. The best-performing model achieved an accuracy of 85.2%, an AUROC of 0.77, and average precision of 0.95, while external validation yielded an accuracy of 83.2% and an AUROC of 0.78. Feature importance analysis using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) indicated that the most influential predictors were the proportion of SPI values exceeding 50, the mean SPI, and the low-to-high frequency power ratio of HRV. Incorporating demographic features, such as age and sex, improved prediction accuracy by up to 5.91%. This improvement may be attributed to the mitigation of inter- and intra-individual variability inherent in biosignals, thereby enhancing the model’s stability and generalizability. Conclusion: ML models incorporating ECG- and PPG-derived features demonstrated reliable prediction of postoperative pain across independent hospital cohorts, highlighting the potential of biosignal-based approaches for objective pain assessment in clinical practice. Clinical trial number: Not applicable. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: The rapid spread of fake news through social media and the internet poses a major challenge, especially in developing countries like Bangladesh. Fake news, consisting of misleading or fabricated content, can severely impact individuals, organizations, and society. Many existing detection methods are complex and resource-intensive, making them unsuitable for real-time applications or low-resource languages. To address this issue, we proposed an efficient fake news detection system using a stacked machine learning model with TF-IDF for feature extraction. TF-IDF converts text into a numerical representation by emphasizing key terms, while the stacked ensemble model improves classification accuracy by integrating multiple machine learning algorithms. We also explored various machine learning classifiers, as well as the mBERT and Word2Vec feature vectorization techniques. We evaluated our system using three datasets: one English and two Bangla fake news detection datasets. The TF-IDF + Stacking model achieved 99.6% accuracy and a 99.8% F1-score on the English dataset. For the first Bangla dataset, accuracy improved from 74.8 to 85.2% after applying SMOTE. On the second Bangla dataset, BanFakeNews, the model achieved 98.4% accuracy, demonstrating strong performance across languages. This research introduces a lightweight yet highly effective machine learning-based fake news detection system, making it suitable for real-world applications, especially in multilingual and resource-limited settings. © The Author(s) 2025.

text: This study aims to explore the application effect of the Transformer model and reinforcement learning optimization framework in the assessment and training of athletes’ psychological quality. By constructing a multi-level psychological quality assessment index system and integrating three types of multimodal data—psychological measurements (e.g., emotional state, anxiety level), physiological signals (e.g., heart rate variability, skin conductance), and behavioral data (e.g., reaction time, action frequency)—we developed a Transformer-based model that captures temporal dependencies and cross-modal feature interactions to accurately assess athletes’ psychological states. The experimental results show that compared with traditional methods, the Transformer model significantly improves the accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score in classification tasks by 8.3%, 8.0%, 8.0% and 8.0% respectively. In addition, by introducing the reinforcement learning optimization framework and personalizing the training program, the athletes’ psychological quality has been significantly improved, with an average improvement of 12%, and key indicators such as emotional stability, anxiety level and concentration have also been significantly improved. Long-term tracking analysis further verifies the effectiveness and durability of the optimization training, providing solid data support for future research and practical applications. © The Author(s) 2025.

text: Advanced persistent threat (APT) attribution is a key defense strategy that can effectively safeguard the security of critical assets and systems. Cyber threat intelligence (CTI) contains rich information about APT groups that can be leveraged for attribution. However, most existing studies focus on a single feature from different perspectives, neglecting the multi-level mining and combined features of CTI, which limits the depth and accuracy of attribution analysis and may even lead to misleading conclusions. To overcome these limitations, we propose a multi-level feature Dempster–Shafer joint (MLDSJ) attribution method for APT groups based on threat intelligence. Specifically, we extract multi-level features such as attack patterns, textual information, and graph topology from CTI reports to construct feature vectors. Subsequently, we classify the three types of features separately using simple machine learning models. Finally, we introduce Dempster–Shafer (DS) evidence theory and apply the Dempster combination rule to integrate the three feature types and determine the final attribution. Experimental results show that our method outperforms the baseline in classification, achieving an accuracy of 89.9%, a recall of 86.5%, and an F1-score of 88.2%. These findings highlight the value of multi-level feature fusion in enhancing APT attribution performance and provide new insights into the design of intelligence-driven defense strategies. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: Parkinson’s disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects both motor and non-motor functions, with speech impairment being one of its earliest and most prevalent symptoms. Detecting PD through voice analysis offers a non-invasive, accessible, and cost-effective approach for early diagnosis and disease monitoring. In this study, we propose a robust deep-learning framework for PD detection using acoustic features extracted from speech recordings. Utilizing the publicly available MDVR-KCL dataset, the recordings are first preprocessed through a voice detection algorithm to isolate relevant speech segments. These segments are then concatenated and divided into fixed 2-second windows from which 43 acoustic features are extracted, including Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients, pitch, formants, spectral, and harmonic descriptors. The resulting features serve as input to various recurrent neural network architectures, namely long short-term memory (LSTM), gated recurrent unit, and bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM), augmented with attention mechanisms to enhance temporal focus. Experimental results show that the BiLSTM-Attention model outperforms all others, achieving an average classification accuracy of 99.85% over 5-fold cross-validation. Statistical analyses confirm the discriminative power of the extracted features. These findings highlight the effectiveness of combining intelligent preprocessing, acoustic signal analysis, and attention-based deep learning to support accurate and scalable voice-based PD diagnostics. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Austria, part of Springer Nature 2026.
label: human

text: Poor posture, particularly during prolonged sitting, is a significant health concern, contributing to musculoskeletal disorders, chronic pain, and reduced quality of life. With nearly 40% of children and many adults affected, there is a pressing need for accessible, user-friendly solutions to monitor and correct posture in real-time. Traditional systems often rely on costly or uncomfortable wearable devices, limiting their practicality. This paper introduces ErgoGuard, a cost-effective, non-wearable posture monitoring system designed to provide real-time feedback and promote healthier sitting habits. The study aims to develop a scalable solution using Force Sensing Resistors (FSRs) and machine learning to classify sitting postures accurately. ErgoGuard integrates ten FSRs on a chair’s seat pan and backrest, connected to a Raspberry Pi 4 for data processing. Pressure distribution data was collected from five participants of diverse age groups and body sizes in various sitting positions (upright, slouched, crossed-leg). The data was preprocessed, normalized, and analyzed using models like Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting Classifier. Results show ErgoGuard achieves robust posture classification, with Random Forest achieving the highest macro-average F1 of 0.87, indicating strong performance across multiple posture classes. The non-wearable design ensures user comfort, while machine learning enables scalable, accurate monitoring. In conclusion, ErgoGuard offers a practical, innovative solution for posture correction, addressing limitations of existing systems. Its cost-effective design and real-time feedback make it suitable for ergonomics, healthcare, and workplace wellness. Future work will explore heat maps to enhance classification performance and provide deeper insights into posture transitions, laying the foundation for advanced, user-centric systems. © The Author(s) 2025.

text: Access to real data is a challenge for research and professional analysis in the insurance sector, especially since such access is often restricted due to confidentiality and competitive issues, particularly in health insurance. This paper introduces a new dataset from a Spanish health insurance portfolio, covering the years 2017 to 2019 with over 70 thousand unique insured and more than 225 thousand rows of data. The dataset contains 42 variables, of which 27 are directly sourced from the insurer and the rest are derived to include area-based contextual information obtained from publicly available sources. The data are anonymized to ensure privacy while maintaining the integrity required for robust professional analysis. Researchers can use the dataset to explore health insurance dynamics, from product design to contextual effects and risk management. Moreover, it supports academic applications for students and educators where they can use real-world data for exercises in data cleaning, statistical analysis and machine learning models. © The Author(s) 2025.

text: This paper introduces the Trauma THOMPSON dataset, designed to advance AI-driven decision support for life-saving interventions (LSIs) in emergency care, particularly in resource constrained humanitarian settings. The dataset comprises 3,717 high resolution and egocentric video clips of both regular and just-in-time (JIT) procedures. The JIT procedures consists of videos of the same LSI procedures, but with makeshift tools, and is useful for studying human medical commonsense. Each clip is annotated by medical professionals with verb-noun format, such as “take scalpel” and “make incision”. In addition to action segments, the dataset includes annotations for medical visual question answering (MVQA), hand maneuvers, and object detection. Eventually, these rich annotations and dataset can be used to train an AI agent to advise first-responders in the field about what to do next with the resources at hand. We provide benchmarks for action recognition, anticipation, and MVQA using state-of-the-art machine learning models. © The Author(s) 2025.

text: Multiple sequence alignment (MSA) is a fundamental technique in computational biology that compares protein, DNA, or RNA sequences to identify regions of similarity reflecting functional, structural, or evolutionary relationships. This systematic literature review examines the diverse land-scape of multiple sequence alignment algorithms, categorizing them based on their underlying approaches and analyzing their strengths, limitations, and applications. We explore seven major categories of alignment methods: dynamic programming, progressive alignment, iterative refinement, Hidden Markov Model-based, consistency-based, structure-based, and machine learning-based approaches. Through comprehensive analysis of recent benchmarks and literature, we identify key innovations, performance characteristics, and emerging trends in the field. This review provides a detailed overview of the evolution of multiple sequence alignment algorithms and their applications in modern bioinformatics. © The Author(s) 2026.
label: human

text: Reliable machine learning techniques have vast potential in assisting clinical decision-making, including applications in bioinformatics and medical imaging analysis. However, AI-driven medical research is often limited by data scarcity, data quality, and the black-box nature of machine learning models. Thus, there is an urgent need for reliable surrogate models to overcome these challenges, enabling accurate learning from small datasets to guide clinical diagnosis. Here, we conducted a retrospective observational clinical study and proposed a data-driven predictive model that estimates mean pulmonary artery pressure (mPAP) based on individual patient clinical diagnostic features, enabling accurate assessment of pulmonary hypertension. Furthermore, we innovatively incorporate CMR-related features into the disease evaluation framework. Compared to traditional invasive measurement methods, this framework can not only accurately predict a patient’s mPAP using easily accessible noninvasive physiological features but also incorporate uncertainty quantification to extract qualitative patterns, aiding clinical diagnosis. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: Universal machine-learning interatomic potentials (uMLIPs) are emerging as foundation models for atomistic simulation, offering near-ab initio accuracy at far lower cost. Their safe, broad deployment is limited by the absence of reliable, general uncertainty estimates. We present a unified, scalable uncertainty metric, U, built from a heterogeneous ensemble that reuses existing pretrained MLIPs. Across diverse chemistries and structures, U strongly tracks true prediction errors and robustly ranks configuration-level risk. Using U, we perform uncertainty-aware distillation to train system-specific potentials with far fewer labels: for tungsten, we match full density-functional-theory (DFT) training using 4% of the DFT data; for MoNbTaW, a dataset distilled by U supports high-accuracy potential training. By filtering numerical label noise, the distilled models can in some cases exceed the accuracy of the MLIPs trained on DFT data. This framework provides a practical reliability monitor and guides data selection and fine-tuning, enabling cost-efficient, accurate, and safer deployment of foundation models. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: The traditional design of single-crystal superalloys relies heavily on trial-and-error experimentation, which is time-consuming and costly. Here, we present an intelligent alloy design strategy that integrates natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning (ML). A domain-specific NLP model was developed to automatically extract γ′ solvus temperature data from scientific literature, enabling the construction of a high-quality database. Machine learning models trained on this data accurately predict both γ′ solvus temperature and creep life. Guided by these models, we screened over 340000 virtual compositions and successfully designed a new low-cost alloy, CSU-S1. Experimental validation shows that CSU-S1 achieves a γ′ solvus temperature near 1300 °C and a creep life of 224.7 h at 1100 °C/137 MPa, comparable to third-generation single-crystal superalloys, while using only 3.1 wt% Re and costing just 121 USD/kg. This work not only delivers a high-performance, cost-effective superalloy but also demonstrates a generalizable “knowledge-to-innovation” design paradigm, offering a powerful new route to accelerate the development of advanced engineering materials. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: We propose a novel approach for constructing training databases for Machine-Learned Interatomic Potential (MLIP) models, specifically designed to capture phase properties across a wide range of conditions. The framework is uniquely appealing due to its ease of automation, its suitability for iterative learning, and its independence from prior knowledge of stable phases, avoiding bias towards pre-existing structural data. The approach uses Nested Sampling (NS) to explore the configuration space and generate thermodynamically relevant configurations, forming the database which undergoes ab initio Density Functional Theory (DFT) evaluation. We use the Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) architecture to fit a model on the resulting database. To demonstrate the efficiency of the framework, we apply it to magnesium, developing a model capable of accurately describing behaviour across pressure and temperature ranges of 0–600 GPa and 0–8000 K, respectively. We benchmark the model’s performance by calculating phonon spectra and elastic constants, as well as the pressure-temperature phase diagram within this region. The results showcase the power of the framework to produce robust MLIPs while maintaining transferability and generality, for reduced computational cost. text: Ancient glass beads excavated across Japan are key materials for understanding cultural exchanges across the Eurasian continent. Conventional analysis relies on expert inspection and chemical composition measurements, making large-scale investigations difficult. To address this issue, we propose a machine learning framework for classifying ancient artifacts using only image data. To correctly classify ancient glass beads based on their provenance, capturing both fine surface details and overall shapes is essential. We achieve this by integrating convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) into a single architecture and employing dual-view (Top and Side) image fusion to emulate expert observation. Furthermore, our framework incorporates bidirectional knowledge distillation between models and viewpoints to ensure feature consistency and efficient multiview fusion. Experiments demonstrate that although the proposed method does not always achieve the highest average accuracy, it exhibits the lowest performance variance across folds, indicating stable classification performance even for rare classes. This study demonstrates for the first time that an image-only approach is effective for the provenance estimation of ancient glass beads. Our findings demonstrate the potential of image-based approaches for analyzing archaeological materials and highlight their promise for advancing archaeological research. © The Author(s) 2026.

text: During global digital transformation, advancing digital infrastructure has emerged as a catalyst for green development in China. Leveraging the Broadband China Pilot policy as a quasi-natural experiment for digital infrastructure construction, this study employs double machine learning models and panel data of 285 Chinese cities from 2010 to 2022 to evaluate the policy’s impact on urban green innovation. Results demonstrate that digital infrastructure development significantly enhances both the quantity and quality of urban green innovation. These findings withstand robustness checks, including sample adjustments and model respecification. Mechanistic analyses reveal that digital infrastructure facilitates innovators’ access to environmental, technological, and market-related information, enabling more targeted and cost-effective green innovation. Heterogeneity analyses indicate a stronger policy effect on high-quality green innovation in eastern, western, and non-resource-dependent cities. This study provides critical insights for leveraging digital infrastructure to advance sustainable economic development through green innovation. © The Author(s) 2026.

text: Due to the growing interconnection and sensitive nature of IoT applications, this article provides a comprehensive overview of cybersecurity in 5G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) networks. The review highlights the significant potential of 5G technology while also emphasizing the need for ongoing work to mitigate its weaknesses and fully harness its advantages. In this context, prospective research avenues are crucial to enhance the resilience and security of 5G-enabled systems. The future directions outlined in this document are diverse and seek to tackle significant deficiencies in 5G-IoT cybersecurity research. A primary objective is to align theoretical analyses with practical implementations by examining specific assaults in IoT contexts, thus improving the comprehension of real-world vulnerabilities. Furthermore, as the 5G standard progresses through its implementation, continuous research must remain synchronized with improvements, focusing on components and features that are currently in development. Formulating effective protection and mitigation strategies to address sophisticated threats is essential for safeguarding this integrated environment. Furthermore, enhancing standards and regulatory frameworks is essential to ensure uniform security procedures across various businesses and areas, especially considering the global nature of 5G-IoT applications. Ultimately, investigating sophisticated attack scenarios that leverage the interaction between IoT devices and 5G infrastructure would enhance understanding of vulnerabilities and aid in the development of more resilient security measures. These directives underscore the necessity for ongoing, flexible research to guarantee the robustness and safety of 5G-enabled IoT networks. © The Author(s) 2026.

text: In today’s volatile financial markets, flexible asset allocation and portfolio management are crucial for maximizing returns and lowering risk. Real-time portfolio allocation optimization is achieved by the Reinforcement Learning-based Adaptive Portfolio Management (RL-APM) system using the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) approach. A state vector representing volatility, recent returns, and current portfolio weights across a range of equity assets is produced by RL-APM, which also analyzes daily market data, including Open, High, Low, Close, and Volume (OHLCV) values, and calculates technical indicators such as SMA, RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands. This state is used by the DDPG agent to create continuous portfolio weights that dynamically adapt to market fluctuations. In contrast to traditional DDPG-based techniques, the method introduces a novel multi-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes volatility, Sharpe ratio, cumulative return, and transaction costs, improving stability and realism. RL-APM outperformed benchmark models like Mean-Variance Optimization (MVO) using daily S&P 500 index data from 2015 to 2024. Average returns increased by 12.4% and the Sharpe ratio by 15.7%. These results demonstrate that the system may scale efficiently for real-world financial scenarios, such as hedge funds, automated trading, and robo-advisory platforms, while also creating flexible allocation plans and maintaining robust risk management. © The Author(s) 2025.
label: human

text: The reliability and efficiency of aircraft maintenance are critical factors in the aviation industry, with significant implications for safety, cost-effectiveness, and operational performance. Recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) have sparked interest in leveraging its capabilities to revolutionize various aspects of aircraft maintenance, including predictive maintenance, fault diagnosis, and health monitoring. This study undertakes a comprehensive examination of AI applications in aircraft maintenance through a systematic literature review. A thorough search of academic publications yielded a dataset of 696 papers spanning four decades (1984-2024), documenting the integration of AI technologies such as Automated Reasoning and Deep Learning with airworthiness management, health monitoring, and maintenance operations. The analysis identifies key AI technologies, associated aircraft maintenance processes, emerging trends, prominent research venues, and underexplored areas. Ultimately, this review provides insights into the adoption of AI in aircraft maintenance, highlighting potential implications for researchers, practitioners, educators, and other stakeholders seeking to optimize maintenance practices through innovative AI-based solutions.

text: This study examines the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in online discussion forums to support autonomous learners. As AI becomes increasingly prevalent in education, it is essential to investigate its impact on the learning experience. A systematic review was conducted using the SALSA framework and PRISMA flow diagram to identify 12 articles that employed AI in discussion forums. The extracted articles covered six applications of AI, including intelligent tutoring systems, chatbots, and automatic formative assessment. Three types of man-machine partnerships were observed: student-AI, student-AI-student, and student-AI-teacher. Analysis revealed that cognitive presence was more predominant than social presence, with a focus on individual learning achievements. Three perspectives emerged regarding the role of AI in learning: supplementing human teaching functions, facilitating information collection, and providing automatic evaluation and feedback. The study also highlighted the importance of communication in acquiring knowledge through AI-mediated interactions. The findings demonstrate that AI is utilized in various forms, including chatbots and generative AI, to support student learning and facilitate connections between students and teachers. Overall, this research contributes to our understanding of the complex interplay between technology, pedagogy, and the learning environment, known as entangled pedagogies.
label: llm

text:
This study aims to transform traditional reactive maintenance strategies in infrastructure and facilities management into proactive, data-driven approaches leveraging advancements in artificial intelligence. Conventional maintenance methods often rely on fixed schedules or post-failure interventions, which are inadequate for mitigating unexpected downtimes and escalating costs. To address these limitations, this research applies multiple machine learning algorithms to a comprehensive synthetic predictive maintenance dataset that encapsulates key operational metrics such as temperature, torque, rotational speed, and tool wear across diverse failure modes.

The objective of this study is to evaluate the performance of various machine learning models in predicting multiclass failures and identify the most effective approach. The comparative analysis reveals that Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) substantially outperforms alternative models, achieving a remarkable accuracy of 98.9% in multiclass failure prediction, supported by an Area Under the Curve (AUC) nearing 1.0 and F1 scores above 0.98 in both validation and test sets.

The Random Forest Classifier (RFC) and Support Vector Classifier (SVC) closely follow, each delivering precision and recall rates exceeding 95%. Notably, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) provides rapid inference, facilitating real-time applications despite slightly lower accuracy metrics (~96.6%). Advanced preprocessing techniques, such as feature scaling, label encoding, and synthetic minority oversampling (SMOTE), enhance model robustness amid inherent class imbalances.

The findings underscore the potential of AI to revolutionize maintenance by offering granular failure diagnostics and enabling timely interventions, thus significantly reducing operational costs and preventing catastrophic infrastructure failures. This research advances the state-of-the-art by integrating interpretability, cross-model benchmarking, and practical scalability considerations, positioning AI-driven predictive maintenance as a cornerstone of modern infrastructure resilience and sustainability.
label: llm

text: Machine learning (ML) methods have shown promise in detecting depression from social media posts; however, their effectiveness in Arabic-speaking populations remains understudied. This research addresses this knowledge gap by introducing the ArabMindGuard (AMG) dataset, comprising 3083 expertly annotated tweets from Saudi Arabia, and systematically comparing ML performance across five datasets representing multiple Arabic dialects, including Saudi, Jordanian, and Modern Standard Arabic (MSA). A rigorous evaluation framework was employed, incorporating precision, recall, F1-score, and statistical significance testing to assess the performance of nine ML classifiers. Advanced Arabic-specific natural language processing techniques were leveraged, including Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), N-gram analysis, and CAMeL tools, to evaluate the efficacy of ensemble methods and Support Vector Machines (SVM). The results indicate that ensemble methods and SVM consistently outperform other approaches, achieving F1-scores ranging from 0.82 to 0.95 across the datasets. These findings highlight the significance of dialectal variation in depression detection and provide foundational baseline metrics for future deep learning applications. This study contributes to culturally responsive digital mental health interventions in the Arab world by establishing methodological benchmarks for Arabic mental health text analysis.
label: llm

text: The integrity and transparency of electoral processes are increasingly dependent on the reliable administration of voting systems, particularly in large-scale elections where timely declaration of results is crucial. However, existing security methods often suffer from limitations such as single points of failure (SPOF) and poor scalability. Moreover, blockchain-based solutions that rely on Proof of Work (PoW) or Proof of Stake (PoS) introduce their own challenges, including reduced throughput, high energy consumption, and vulnerability to emerging post-quantum attacks. Recent instances of electoral misinformation campaigns have highlighted the potential for chaos and compromised voter privacy in contemporary systems. To address these concerns, this study proposes a novel blockchain-based hybrid consensus voting mechanism called Post Quantum Secured Hierarchical Authoritative Consensus (PQSHAC). PQSHAC incorporates an optimized sharding protocol that leverages the distributed nature of election data to reduce skewness and increase shard cohesion. Additionally, a token generation protocol is employed to prevent extraneous voting and ensure voter privacy. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of PQSHAC in achieving high throughput and robust security against quantum computing attacks via Dilithium-3 integration. Notably, the proposed modular ledger architecture reduces storage requirements by approximately 60% compared to non-modular approaches. The findings of this study underscore the strengths of PQSHAC as a secure and efficient solution for improving trust and dependability in contemporary voting systems.
label: llm

text: This study addresses a critical issue in Continuously Reinforced Concrete Pavement (CRCP) infrastructure management, namely transverse cracking, which compromises ride quality, service life, and maintenance strategies. To improve the accuracy of transverse crack prediction, a novel hybrid framework is proposed that integrates Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) with Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM). A comprehensive dataset comprising 395 records from 33 pavement sections in the Long-Term Pavement Performance (LTPP) program was utilized to inform model development. The PSO algorithm was employed to optimize critical GBM hyperparameters, including iteration count, learning rate, and tree depth. Results indicate that the optimized PSO-GBM model exhibits superior performance compared to benchmark models such as conventional GBM, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression (SVR), Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), and Linear Regression, with an average Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 1.62 and a coefficient of determination (R2) of 0.99 under 5-fold cross-validation. Sensitivity analysis revealed that pavement layer thicknesses (L3 and L4) and Annual Average Daily Traffic (AADT) are the most influential factors in transverse crack development, consistent with theoretical expectations. The robustness and stability of the proposed approach were validated through residual distribution plots and equality line analyses across various severity levels.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of misinformation in digital media poses a pressing concern that necessitates effective solutions. Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a promising approach for detecting genuine and fake news. This study develops an enhanced convolutional neural network (CNN) model to improve the accuracy of news authenticity recognition. The proposed CNN model leverages deep learning techniques, which are optimized through iterative refinement. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved CNN model significantly outperforms its counterparts in identifying authentic and fabricated content. Specifically, after 50 iterations, it achieves an impressive accuracy rate of 96.97%, surpassing a baseline model based on random deactivation techniques by 7.29%. The model's superiority is also evident across various datasets, including FakeNewsNet and LIAR. On the former dataset, its normalized mutual information reaches 84.82%, representing a notable improvement over traditional methods (5.05% increase) and models based on random inactivation techniques (10.25% increase). Similarly, on the latter dataset, it achieves an adjusted Rand index of 87.32%. The study's primary contribution lies in harnessing AI technology to develop robust CNN models for mitigating the spread of misinformation, thereby safeguarding societal information security, upholding public right-to-know principles, and promoting the dissemination of accurate news.
label: llm

text: In the realm of intelligent manufacturing, the accurate classification of weld bead defects is a critical challenge in Gas Metal Arc Welding (GMAW) processes, particularly in robotic welding applications. To address this issue, we propose a novel hybrid machine learning approach that synergizes the strengths of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Vision Transformers (ViT). This innovative framework leverages CNN's ability to extract localized features with high precision, while ViT provides global contextual awareness, enabling the model to capture complex relationships between weld bead characteristics. We demonstrate that our hybrid architecture outperforms traditional architectures such as ResNet and conventional CNN models in terms of classification accuracy. The performance evaluation of our proposed model reveals its robustness and reliability across diverse operational scenarios, underscoring the potential of integrating hybrid deep learning models into industrial automation systems to enhance weld bead defect detection. By reducing operational inefficiencies and ensuring consistent weld quality, our findings contribute significantly to the advancement of manufacturing process efficiency in GMAW applications.
label: llm

text: This study proposes an innovative hybrid framework for sentiment analysis specifically designed for the Greek language. By integrating a novel lexicon construction methodology with Active Learning (AL) strategies, we aim to improve performance and reduce annotation effort. A domain-specific lexicon was developed using two custom metrics: Score<inf>w</inf>, which captures the sentiment polarity of words, and Weight<inf>w</inf>, which reflects their combined emotional and statistical importance. These metrics enabled the transformation of tweet-level annotations into semantically rich word-level representations. To evaluate the framework's efficacy, we trained four classification models (Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, SVM, and Decision Trees) with and without lexicon-derived features. The results demonstrate significant improvements in accuracy and F1-score when incorporating the lexicon, particularly for Logistic Regression and Decision Tree models. Furthermore, a dynamic Active Learning approach was employed, where the lexicon was reconstructed in each cycle using the evolving labeled dataset. This strategy achieved comparable levels of accuracy and Macro F1-score to full-supervision, while utilizing only 30% of the data. The findings suggest that combining lexicon enhancement with uncertainty-based sampling offers a cost-effective and scalable solution for sentiment analysis in low-resource languages such as Greek.
label: llm

text: In materials design, machine learning predictors derived from word embeddings of atoms have garnered significant attention in recent years. However, the reliability and robustness of these predictors are contingent upon the variability inherent in their underlying representations. This study investigates whether the static word embeddings affiliated with physicochemical entities exhibit variability due to information heterogeneity within training material. Specifically, we examine the effects of vocabulary term selection on individual atomic embeddings and assess their impact on domain-specialized information transfer. Our analysis reveals substantial variability in atomic embeddings, which is largely influenced by the choice of language modelling terms. Notably, this variability does not preclude the successful mapping of materials' composite predictors into physicochemical properties when joined atomic embeddings are incorporated within a regression model estimating compound stability through formation energy prediction. Furthermore, our results demonstrate that vector calibration via dimensional reduction maintains the encoded information and predictive performance stability. This research provides empirical evidence on the magnitude of variability in word embeddings of physicochemical entities, highlighting their potential to facilitate stable predictive performance despite origin-dependent differences.
label: llm

text:
The development of machine learning models for atomic configurations has given rise to numerous sets of descriptors, yet many of these are plagued by incompleteness or functional dependence. Incomplete descriptor sets fail to accurately capture the complexities of atomic environments, while complete constructions often suffer from redundancy, where certain descriptors can be expressed as functions of others. This redundancy does not contribute to improved discrimination between atomic configurations and instead increases computational complexity.

To address this issue, we employ pattern recognition techniques to identify and remove dependent descriptors, resulting in a minimal set that satisfies completeness. We apply this strategy in two distinct contexts: the refinement of an existing description, the atomic cluster expansion, and the augmentation of an incomplete construction. The latter yields a novel message-passing neural network architecture capable of recognizing up to 5-body patterns.

Our results demonstrate the efficacy of this approach in optimizing descriptor sets across various descriptors and application datasets. Notably, the proposed architecture exhibits strong accuracy on state-of-the-art benchmarks while maintaining low computational costs. These findings underscore the importance of carefully selecting and refining descriptor sets to enhance machine learning model performance.
label: llm

text: Silicon carbide (SiC), a technologically relevant material, exhibits unique properties in high-energy grain boundaries (HEGBs). However, the diffusion mechanism of cesium (Cs) within these HEGBs remains poorly understood due to limitations in computational modeling. Specifically, long-time scale simulations are hindered by the lack of reliable and computationally efficient interatomic potentials. This study addresses this challenge by developing a machine learning framework for constructing moment tensor potentials (MTPs), which enables efficient active training on complex structures. Our approach facilitates the extraction of unknown regions from HEGBs, allowing for the first time simulations of Cs diffusion in both amorphous SiC and HEGBs. The results indicate that Cs diffuses via a cage-breaking mechanism, supported by radial distribution functions, Voronoi analysis, and electronic structure calculations. These findings provide valuable insights into local atomic environments and weak interactions governing Cs mobility. Furthermore, this work presents a generalizable workflow for training machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) on complex structures, offering a significant advancement in modeling fission product behaviors.
label: llm

text:
The ionospheric Spread-F phenomenon has garnered substantial attention in both ionospheric electrodynamic research and radio-based technology applications, including communication and navigation systems. However, the classification of Spread-F remains largely dependent on manual interpretation of ionograms by experts, which is plagued by inefficiencies (~10 seconds per figure) and subjective biases. Currently, there is a lack of publicly available ionogram datasets that classify Frequency/Range/Mix/Strong Range SF (FSF/RSF/MSF/SSF) through either human labor or machine processing.

To address this limitation, we present an expert-guided ionogram dataset that represents the most comprehensive collection in terms of class coverage, the largest volume, and the most extensive temporal span to date. This dataset comprises 150,000 ionograms (30,000 per class, including a "non-SF" group) spanning 14 years from 2002 to 2016, thereby capturing a diverse range of solar and geomagnetic conditions. Based on this dataset, we develop a classification model using a deep learning architecture that can be applied to further data, facilitating more efficient and objective classification of ionograms. This study aims to bridge the gap between manual interpretation and automated processing in ionospheric research, ultimately contributing to enhanced operational applications in radio-based technologies.
label: llm

text: The accuracy of current survival prediction models for extra-nodal natural killer/T-cell lymphoma (ENKTL) is suboptimal, necessitating the development of more effective predictive tools. This study aimed to address this limitation by designing and validating a machine learning-based algorithm that integrates clinical and pathological covariates to predict patient outcomes. A comprehensive dataset comprising 977 subjects from four cohorts was analyzed to evaluate model performance using a range of metrics, including Harrell's c-index, receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves, calibration curves, and decision curve analysis (DCA). The results indicate that the Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM) outperformed other machine learning algorithms tested in this study, leading to the development of the ENKTL-ML score. The performance of the ENKTL-ML score was evaluated across multiple cohorts, yielding c-index values ranging from 0.82 to 0.84, indicating a high degree of accuracy. Furthermore, comparison with established models (IPI, KPI, PINK-E, and NRI) revealed that the ENKTL-ML score significantly outperformed these benchmarks in predicting survival outcomes. This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning-based approaches in improving the prognostic accuracy for patients with ENKTL, facilitating more informed clinical decision-making.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of electric vehicles has sparked a pressing need for accurate estimation of remaining driving range, particularly in cargo transportation applications. This study addresses this challenge by developing a machine learning-based approach to predict the remaining driving range of cargo electric vehicles (CEVs). A comprehensive dataset was generated through real-world testing of a Musoshi Pop-Up Mini electric cargo vehicle on a 2 km campus route under varying load and speed conditions, allowing for high-resolution modeling of energy consumption patterns. The dataset underwent systematic preprocessing, including feature engineering and segment-wise aggregation, to prepare it for analysis. Seven regression algorithms – ElasticNet, Support Vector Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost, CatBoost, and ExtraTrees – were evaluated using Optuna-based Bayesian hyperparameter tuning and compared based on their performance metrics (RMSE, MAE, R²). Among the tested models, the Support Vector Regression (SVR) model yielded the most accurate predictions, with an RMSE of 2.37, MAE of 1.75, and R² of 0.892. These results demonstrate that data-driven approaches can effectively estimate energy consumption and driving range for CEVs, thereby facilitating safer and more reliable deployment of electric mobility systems.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of e-commerce has dramatically altered consumer behavior, necessitating the development of sophisticated predictive models for digital marketing strategies. This study investigates the potential of integrating machine learning (ML) models with Structural Equation Modelling (SEM) to forecast consumer purchase intentions. A comprehensive dataset comprising consumer reviews and behavioral signals from prominent e-commerce platforms is employed to examine the efficacy of various ML algorithms, including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Networks, in conjunction with sentiment analysis and TF-IDF text vectorization. Furthermore, a SEM framework is proposed and validated to link consumer trust, product quality perception, and sentiment polarity to buying intentions. The results demonstrate that ensemble ML models outperform linear classifiers and provide theoretical grounding by validating latent constructs. Notably, the hybrid SEM-ML framework achieves both predictive accuracy and theoretical rigor. This research offers practical insights for e-commerce managers seeking to optimize customer engagement strategies. To enhance theoretical clarity and robustness, the SEM is anchored within established theories, including the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and Theory of Planned Behaviour (TPB), with testable hypotheses specified. Sentiment analysis is conducted using both VADER and DistilBERT models, and validated on a manually annotated subsample. Time-aware validation and a robustness check are also performed to ensure the reliability of the findings.
label: llm

text:
Here is a rewritten abstract based on the source text:

The accurate identification and diagnosis of mental disorders and learning disabilities are crucial for timely intervention, yet traditional assessments often fall short in terms of efficiency and effectiveness. Recent advances in machine learning and feature selection techniques hold promise for improving these outcomes, but their application to large transdiagnostic datasets and quick assessment tools remains underexplored. This study addresses this gap by applying machine learning and feature selection methods to a comprehensive dataset featuring over 1,000 questionnaire items from the Healthy Brain Network (n = 4,136). A two-stage feature selection approach was employed to identify optimal item subsets for assessing various disorders and symptoms, as well as custom outcome measures for learning disabilities. The performance of these models was then compared to existing assessments through rigorous cross-validation procedures. Our results demonstrate that machine learning models using parsimonious item subsets significantly outperform traditional assessments (p = 0.004), with AUC values reaching up to 0.855 for specific learning disorders. Notably, restricting analysis to non-proprietary assessment items did not compromise performance. This study showcases the potential of leveraging existing datasets to develop efficient and effective assessment tools for mental health professionals, highlighting the importance of open-source software architectures that facilitate adaptation to diverse datasets while emphasizing the need for external validation prior to clinical implementation.
label: llm

text: The increasing prevalence of myopia poses a significant global health challenge, necessitating innovative approaches to its management. To address this concern, we developed the Myopia Progression Predictive Model (MPPM), an artificial intelligence framework based on the Transformer architecture. This model comprises two primary components: the Natural Progression Module (NPM) and the Intervention Progression Module (IPM). NPM was trained on a comprehensive dataset of refractive records from over 304,000 children and adolescents, resulting in high predictive accuracy for spherical equivalent (SE) and axial length (AL) progression over a decade. Specifically, internal testing revealed strong correlations between predicted and actual values, with SE predictions achieving an R² value of 0.94 and mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.35 diopters, while AL predictions reached R² = 0.91 and MAE = 0.16 millimeters. The model's performance was also validated in external cohorts. Building on this foundation, IPM was designed to forecast the effects of specific interventions, including atropine therapy, orthokeratology, peripheral defocus spectacles, and repeated low-level red light (RLRL) therapy. By leveraging a causal machine learning framework, MPPM enables individualized predictions of treatment outcomes, with SE and AL predictions demonstrating high accuracy across all interventions. Notably, RLRL therapy was found to slightly reverse myopia progression, while the other interventions slowed its advancement. The MPPM model holds considerable promise as an AI-driven platform for optimizing pediatric myopia management, offering a personalized approach to predicting and mitigating the effects of this prevalent condition.
label: llm

text: The production of nanofibers through electrospinning is a complex process influenced by multiple parameters, including process conditions, environmental factors, and solution properties. As a result, achieving optimal nanofiber quality requires extensive experimentation and fine-tuning. However, the scarcity of publicly available datasets hinders modeling efforts and limits the establishment of reference parameters for stable nanofiber formation. To address this challenge, we have developed Cogni-e-Spin DB 1.0, a comprehensive dataset comprising 809 experimental records of electrospinning parameters and corresponding nanofiber morphologies. This dataset is designed to provide a large-scale, diverse, and machine-learning-ready resource for researchers seeking to understand the complex relationships between process conditions and nanofiber properties. Our dataset enables thorough analyses of process-structure-property interactions and supports the application of machine learning methods to gain insights into electrospinning phenomena. To validate the dataset's reliability and utility, we have verified its integrity against original publications and demonstrated its effectiveness through proof-of-concept machine learning modeling. The Cogni-e-Spin DB 1.0 dataset is intended to accelerate fundamental research, model development, and process optimization in various electrospinning applications, while a companion web platform facilitates live contributions and interactive exploration of the data, promoting continuous community-driven updates.
label: llm

text:
Recent advancements in machine learning have revolutionized the field of cancer cell classification, with a growing emphasis on leveraging high-resolution microscopy images to extract informative features. However, traditional methods often rely heavily on manual preprocessing and handcrafted feature extraction, which can introduce user bias and limit scalability. This study addresses these limitations by developing an automated, interpretable, and organelle-focused deep learning framework for classifying breast cancer cell lines from fluorescence microscopy images.

The proposed framework employs a novel end-to-end approach that incorporates patch-based sampling, sparsity filtering, and channel-wise intermediate fusion to extract and integrate organelle-specific features. To ensure model interpretability, Grad-CAM visualizations and single-organelle classifier analyses were employed to examine the relationships between different organelles and classification decisions.

The framework was evaluated on a dataset comprising fluorescence microscopy images from six breast cancer cell lines using 5-fold cross-validation, yielding a classification accuracy of 97.1±1.1%. Notably, this performance surpassed or matched conventional handcrafted feature-based approaches while eliminating the need for manual segmentation and 3D rendering steps. Further analysis revealed that mitochondria played a crucial role in classification decisions, underscoring the importance of organelle morphology and spatial organization.

The proposed framework offers a scalable, automated, and interpretable deep learning solution that has significant implications for microscopy-based phenotyping and computational pathology. By leveraging organelle-specific features, this study demonstrates the potential for improved cancer cell classification accuracy and sheds light on the underlying mechanisms driving classification decisions.
label: llm

text: The incidence of accidental falls among individuals aged 65 and older has become a pressing public health concern due to its high frequency and severe consequences. Such falls can lead to fractures, traumatic brain injuries, and long-term complications if timely intervention is not provided. In response, researchers have been developing automated fall detection systems that integrate intelligent algorithms with sensor-based data acquisition to facilitate rapid response and medical assistance. This study undertakes a systematic literature review within the PRISMA framework to examine the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) approaches in fall detection. A comprehensive search across major databases yielded 182 relevant articles after removing duplicates and applying strict inclusion/exclusion criteria from an initial pool of 596 articles. The review encompasses a wide range of AI/ML techniques applied to fall detection using diverse sensor modalities, including wearable, vision-based, ambient, and multimodal systems. Furthermore, it summarizes the publicly available datasets and sensor configurations used in existing studies. This comprehensive perspective enables comparison of trade-offs across sensing modalities and informs effective system development for real-world deployment. The review provides a comparative overview of AI/ML-based fall detection approaches, highlighting differences in accuracy, sensitivity, and dataset usage as reported in the literature. Moreover, it outlines several open research challenges in using AI and ML to detect falls and presents potential future perspectives for advancing this field.
label: llm

text: Retinitis pigmentosa (RP) is a hereditary retinal degeneration characterized by night blindness, progressive loss of peripheral vision, and eventual blindness. Current treatments, including gene therapy and retinal prosthetics, are limited in their applicability due to the complexity and heterogeneity of RP. To address this challenge, we developed a diagnostic and prognostic model for RP utilizing large-scale deep learning (DL) architectures pre-trained on fundus image datasets. Our results indicate that the EfficientNetB4 model achieves an Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC) of 0.94 in diagnosing RP, with improved performance in patients with preserved vision. For visual prognosis, we employed machine learning survival analysis to integrate DL-derived image features and clinical metadata, adopting a strict patient-level split to mitigate data leakage. Notably, our hybrid model combining imaging and clinical data outperformed models relying on either modality alone, particularly among female patients. Time-dependent AUC analysis revealed that prognostic performance peaked between 500 and 1400 days post-examination. SHAP-based interpretability analysis further elucidated the distinct features contributing to RP diagnosis and prognosis. While our study demonstrates the value of fundus images in predicting visual outcomes, external validation using diverse datasets is essential for translating these findings into clinical practice.
label: llm

text: In the realm of electrocardiogram (ECG) signal processing, accurate and efficient classification is crucial for real-time medical applications and wearable devices. However, existing methods face significant challenges due to noise contamination, limited labeled data, and high model complexity. To address these issues, we propose a novel Quantum Angle-Distance Kernel (QADK) framework that leverages quantum-inspired principles to enhance class resolution while maintaining transparency. QADK represents heartbeats as descriptive features mapped into fixed-length complex vectors using a simulated quantum coding scheme. The encoded patterns are then compared using a balanced mixture of similarity scores and projection gaps, generating a stable kernel matrix compatible with conventional machine learning tools. We evaluate the performance of QADK on a large ECG dataset through 100 Monte-Carlo measurements, comparing it to established methods such as linear SVM, RBF SVM, 1D CNN, MLP, and single-class anomaly detection. Our results demonstrate that QADK-SVM achieves state-of-the-art classification accuracy, with an average AUC-ROC of 0.977 ± 0.003, macro F1 score of 0.923 ± 0.005, and accuracy of 0.937 ± 0.004. Furthermore, we show that both components of the QADK pipeline contribute to its performance, with the fidelity term primarily improving specificity. Theoretical analysis reveals that QADK is compatible with near-term quantum hardware, requiring only 11 qubits and standard swap tests. This makes it an attractive solution for ECG classification in resource-constrained environments, where strong accuracy, high interpretability, and efficient computation are essential.
label: llm

text:
Here is a rewritten abstract for the machine learning study:

Osteoporosis poses a significant public health concern in aging populations, with diagnosis often relying on dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA), a costly and radiation-based method. This study aimed to develop an interpretable machine learning model that can predict osteoporosis risk using readily available demographic, biochemical, and lifestyle data. The proposed model was designed as an accessible online calculator for rapid clinical and community screening.

To develop the model, we leveraged the Korean National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (KNHANES) dataset from 2008-2011, excluding individuals with over 30% missing data and imputing incomplete variables using polynomial interpolation and mode imputation. Spearman correlation analysis identified osteoporosis-related features, which were then reduced to 15 essential predictors through GradientBoost-RFE and LASSO regression. We trained GradientBoost, CatBoost, and XGBoost algorithms on these predictors to estimate abnormal DXA results and classify bone status.

Our model was evaluated using the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC), specificity (SPE), and accuracy (ACC) metrics, with a temporal validation set from the 2008 wave of KNHANES for testing. The optimal model achieved an AUC of 0.845 and SPE of 0.897 for identifying abnormal DXA outcomes, and demonstrated high performance in multiclass classification tasks.

We conclude that our interpretable machine learning model accurately predicts osteoporosis risk and DXA abnormalities using readily available data. To facilitate clinical translation, the model has been deployed as an interactive online calculator, enabling non-invasive, rapid osteoporosis risk assessment without radiological testing. This tool may support early identification of high-risk individuals, optimize DXA utilization, and enhance preventive care strategies across diverse healthcare settings.
label: llm

text:
The deployment of unsupervised anomaly detection systems in diverse smart home environments is often hampered by the requirement for time-consuming and costly hyperparameter tuning at each site. To address this critical challenge, we investigate the concept of hyperparameter transferability as a means of creating plug-and-play security solutions that require minimal setup. This study systematically evaluates the performance of five prominent unsupervised machine learning models – Elliptic Envelope, Isolation Forest, Local Outlier Factor, One-Class SVM, and Autoencoder – across five widely used IoT datasets.

Our evaluation methodology involves rigorous dataset-specific hyperparameter tuning to establish a baseline for each model's performance. We then compare the results of transferred configurations against both per-dataset optimization and default settings. Our findings indicate that while optimal performance is still achieved through dataset-specific tuning, an intelligent transfer strategy significantly outperforms default configurations in many cases.

Notably, our analysis reveals that the IoTID20 dataset serves as a particularly effective source for hyperparameter transfer due to its high feature space complexity and cluster overlap, which create a challenging yet robust training environment. Furthermore, we identify a strategic trade-off between model performance and ease of use: while Autoencoders and Local Outlier Factor deliver outstanding absolute performance, Isolation Forest offers the most substantial improvement over default settings.

This research provides a quantitative framework for dataset-driven initialization, offering insights that can inform the development of robust, low-maintenance intrusion detection systems. By identifying effective hyperparameter transfer strategies, our study aims to facilitate the widespread adoption of anomaly detection systems in smart home environments.
label: llm

text: The early and accurate detection of Alzheimer's disease (AD) remains a critical clinical challenge. Recent advances in machine learning have enabled the development of novel diagnostic models that leverage blood transcriptomic data to identify individuals at risk of developing AD. This study presents an innovative approach, the Lactylation-Derived Score (LDS), which integrates lactylation-related genes from nine AD cohorts using a standardized pipeline incorporating z-score normalization, random forest-based feature selection, plsRglm modeling, and 10-fold cross-validation. The LDS model was externally validated on seven independent brain transcriptomic datasets and clinically evaluated in an external plasma cohort comprising 540 samples. Logistic regression was employed to combine LDS with plasma phosphorylated tau 181 (p-tau181) and p-tau217 biomarkers, yielding a three-marker model that achieved superior diagnostic performance compared to individual components. The results demonstrate the potential of LDS as a scalable and interpretable transcriptomic model for AD diagnosis, complementing existing plasma-based biomarkers and supporting precision medicine approaches in this field. Notably, LDS-high individuals exhibited distinct neuroinflammatory activation and metabolic stress signatures, underscoring its utility in identifying at-risk populations and informing targeted therapeutic interventions.
label: llm

text:
Recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI), particularly machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL), have revolutionized various fields, including dentistry. The increasing complexity of patient data and the need for more efficient clinical decision-making processes have prompted the development of AI-driven tools to support dental professionals. This study aimed to design and evaluate an AI-powered tool, HistorAI, that analyzes patient anamnesis forms to generate comprehensive reports and provide treatment suggestions.

To assess the effectiveness of HistorAI, a 22-item anamnesis questionnaire was used to guide structured prompting of two AI models: GPT-4 and Gemini. The integrated API enabled data analysis, treatment suggestion generation, prescription writing, and referral recommendations. Descriptive statistics and independent-samples t-tests were employed to compare evaluation times and outcomes between AI-assisted and conventional methods. Effect sizes were calculated using Cohen's d, with a significance threshold of p < 0.01.

Results indicate that HistorAI successfully evaluates medical and dental histories, identifies contraindicated medications and anesthetics, assesses patient complaints, and provides preliminary treatment recommendations. Notably, the AI-assisted process significantly reduced evaluation time compared to conventional methods (p < 0.01), with a substantial efficiency gain (Cohen's d = 2.599). These findings suggest that HistorAI can enhance clinical decision-making in dental practice while maintaining clinician oversight.

The integration of AI-powered tools like HistorAI into dental workflows holds promise for improving efficiency and patient care. However, further clinical validation and consideration of ethical implications are necessary to ensure the safe and responsible adoption of these technologies in dental settings.
label: llm

text:
Accurate prediction of novel compound-protein interactions (CPIs) is a critical component of the drug discovery process, enabling researchers to identify potential therapeutic targets with unprecedented speed and efficiency. However, the generalizability of machine learning-based CPI prediction models is severely limited by the availability and diversity of high-quality training datasets. Federated learning (FL), an emerging paradigm for integrating multi-site data while preserving privacy, has been proposed as a solution to this challenge. Nonetheless, its effectiveness in handling heterogeneous data from diverse molecular domains remains poorly understood, hindering its broader adoption in industry settings.

This study investigates the application of FL to CPI prediction using datasets spanning multiple chemical and protein domains, with the aim of providing practical guidance for optimizing the FL approach. Our results indicate that while the FL model exhibits improved out-of-domain prediction performance, it is outperformed by local models on in-domain data under conditions of high data heterogeneity. Building on these findings, we develop a novel strategy to achieve robust performance across both in- and out-of-domain tasks: a similarity-guided ensemble (SGE) that combines the global FL model with fine-tuned models based on each client's local data.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach using real-world industry data, including samples from public databases and proprietary datasets from 13 pharmaceutical companies. Our findings provide practical insights for implementing FL in contemporary drug discovery processes, addressing a critical knowledge gap in the field.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the application of machine learning (ML) techniques for predicting gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) risk among pregnant women. A retrospective analysis was conducted on a dataset comprising 3,649 pregnant women from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) database spanning 2007-2018. The dataset included baseline demographic information and dietary nutrient intake data for 45 nutrients.
