To address class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed after splitting the dataset into training and testing sets. Feature selection was performed using Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) to reduce multicollinearity and the Boruta algorithm to identify core predictors. Six ML models were trained, including XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Gaussian Naive Bayes (GNB), and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). Model performance was evaluated using metrics such as area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC), accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F-Beta score, and precision-recall AUC. SHAP analysis was conducted to elucidate feature importance. The results indicate that XGBoost outperformed other models in predicting GDM risk, achieving an accuracy of 93.1% and AUC of 0.966 in the validation set. Core predictors identified by the study include demographics, dietary nutrients such as protein, fiber, and carotenoids, as well as lifestyle factors like smoking status and educational level. The findings suggest that ML models integrating demographic and nutritional data can accurately predict GDM risk, supporting early detection and intervention in clinical practice.
label: llm

text:
The identification and classification of cell types from single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) datasets pose significant challenges in understanding complex biological systems. Recent advances in scRNA-seq have revealed novel cell types and functions, but the lack of robust annotation methods hinders further discovery. To address this limitation, we developed CIA, a computational tool designed to accurately identify cell types across diverse datasets without reliance on fully annotated reference datasets or intricate machine learning processes.

CIA leverages predefined cell type signatures to provide a user-friendly solution for cell-type and functional annotation of single cells. The framework is implemented in both Python and R programming languages, ensuring compatibility with major single-cell analysis frameworks. This accessibility enables researchers to integrate CIA into their existing workflows with minimal additional effort.

Our evaluation demonstrates that CIA achieves classification performances comparable to state-of-the-art approaches while requiring significantly lower computational resources. Furthermore, CIA facilitates reproducible signature-based cell assignments through graphical summaries, empowering researchers to explore the complex transcriptional landscape of single cells with greater ease and precision. By streamlining cell-type identification and annotation, CIA offers a powerful tool for advancing our understanding of biological systems at the cellular level.
label: llm

text: This study presents MatterVial, an advanced hybrid framework for feature-based machine learning in materials science. By integrating latent representations from a variety of pretrained graph-neural network (GNN) models with computationally efficient descriptors and novel features derived from symbolic regression, MatterVial significantly expands the feature space available to traditional feature-based models. This approach leverages the chemical transparency of traditional methods while harnessing the predictive power of deep learning architectures. When applied to Matbench tasks, MatterVial demonstrates substantial error reductions and enhances the performance of MODNet, a state-of-the-art feature-based model, to rival that of end-to-end GNNs in several instances, with accuracy increases exceeding 40% for multiple tasks. Furthermore, an integrated interpretability module enables the decoding of latent GNN-derived descriptors into explicit, physically meaningful formulas through the use of surrogate models and symbolic regression. By providing a high-performance tool that aligns with explainable AI principles, MatterVial advances materials informatics and facilitates more targeted and autonomous materials discovery.
label: llm

text: The diagnosis and prognosis of Alzheimer's Disease (AD) have been increasingly reliant on artificial intelligence (AI) models that integrate multimodal data from various sources. However, the lack of standardization and transparency in these approaches hinders their interpretability and clinical adoption. This study aims to address this gap by systematically reviewing recent advances in AI-driven multimodal fusion for AD prediction. A comprehensive analysis of widely used datasets is presented, including an examination of their modalities, preprocessing pipelines, and accessibility to ensure reproducibility and methodological transparency. The review categorizes data harmonization and preprocessing techniques across neuroimaging (fMRI, sMRI, PET), electrophysiological (EEG), and genomic modalities, highlighting domain-specific practices and challenges. Fusion strategies are classified into four paradigms: data-level, feature-level, decision-level, and temporal (early, intermediate, late) approaches, which are evaluated for their implementation and diagnostic impact. Furthermore, the adoption of explainable AI (XAI) techniques is investigated across studies, revealing a significant underrepresentation of works that prioritize multimodality, explainability, and methodological rigor. This study adheres to PRISMA and Kitchenham's guidelines to ensure transparency and replicability in evidence synthesis. The review concludes with recommendations for future research aimed at developing robust, interpretable, and clinically relevant AI models for AD diagnosis and prognosis.
label: llm

text: The inverse design of novel materials is increasingly reliant on machine learning-driven approaches to navigate the vast composition space. However, these methods are often hindered by two primary limitations: (i) their tendency to suffer from prediction biases in high-dimensional spaces, and (ii) their inability to effectively incorporate domain expert knowledge into the design process. To address these challenges, we propose AIMATDESIGN, a reinforcement learning framework that leverages difference-based sampling to augment experimental data and construct a robust experience pool. A novel automated refinement strategy, guided by large language models, is employed to correct prediction inconsistencies and ensure alignment between reward signals and value functions. Furthermore, a knowledge-based reward mechanism is integrated to leverage expert rules and enhance stability and efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that AIMATDESIGN outperforms conventional machine learning and reinforcement learning baselines in terms of discovery efficiency, convergence speed, and success rates. Notably, the framework's ability to capture property trends is exemplified by the experimental validation of a Zr-based alloy exhibiting exceptional mechanical properties, including 1.7 GPa yield strength and 10.2% elongation. The proposed framework provides a reliable pathway toward closed-loop, data-efficient materials discovery, underscoring its potential for transformative impact in the field.
label: llm

text:
In the field of electroencephalography (EEG)-based analysis, particularly in neurolinguistics and second language acquisition, feature selection plays a pivotal role in optimizing machine learning models. Understanding neural activity during language learning offers valuable insights into cognitive and linguistic processing. Despite significant advancements in EEG-based studies, identifying the most effective feature selection techniques to enhance classification performance and interpretability remains an open challenge. This study systematically evaluates and compares multiple feature selection methods to improve the accuracy and efficiency of EEG-based classification during the acquisition of Arabic and Hindi vocabulary.

EEG signals were recorded from twenty participants as they learned new words, and the data underwent preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification using a range of machine learning algorithms, including Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree (DT), XGBoost, CatBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN). Five feature selection techniques – Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR), refined selection, ReliefF, ensemble selection, and Genetic Algorithm (GA) – were applied and compared. The results demonstrate that feature selection significantly enhances classification performance, with ensemble and ReliefF achieving the highest accuracies of 97.13% and 97.15%, respectively, particularly when combined with RF, SVM, and XGBoost classifiers.

These findings indicate that effective dimensionality reduction, mitigation of overfitting, and enhanced model interpretability can be achieved through judicious application of feature selection techniques. This systematic evaluation provides valuable insights into neural mechanisms of language learning and supports advancements in brain–computer interfaces, cognitive neuroscience, and machine learning applications.
label: llm

text: In the realm of computational chemistry, a comprehensive understanding of proton transfer mechanisms is crucial for elucidating polar reaction mechanisms. To address this knowledge gap, we have compiled an extensive dataset comprising over 51 million kinetically plausible proton transfer steps between heteroatoms from approximately 8,000 acids and conjugate bases with experimentally determined aqueous pKa values spanning a wide range (−15 to +37). By leveraging a simplified Eigen equation, we estimated rate factors at 25 °C using only pKa values without incorporating statistical factors. Our dataset was further augmented by including an additional 5,043 proton transfer steps from carbon acids to heteroatom bases, calculated using the Eigen-Bernasconi equation and reported intrinsic rate constants along with Brønsted β values. To ensure machine learning compatibility, each entry was encoded in SMIRKS format with electron-flow specification. Prioritizing structural diversity over environmental conditions, our dataset is expected to provide accurate predictions of aqueous proton transfer rates. The compiled dataset offers a valuable resource for training models to predict stepwise mechanistic pathways, thereby enhancing the understanding of polar reaction mechanisms and facilitating the development of more accurate computational tools in chemistry.
label: llm

text:
In the realm of biological and biomedical research, elucidating the relationships between metabolites and biochemical pathways is crucial for interpreting molecular datasets. However, pathway annotations are often scarce in these datasets, hindering their utility for comprehensive analysis. To address this limitation, previous studies have leveraged machine learning models to predict pathway associations using data from the Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes (KEGG). Nevertheless, other knowledgebases like MetaCyc offer a vast repository of compound entries and pathway definitions, warranting exploration.

This study extends the existing research by developing and evaluating multilayer perceptron models on compound entries and pathway annotations derived from MetaCyc. The results demonstrate that these models achieve a mean Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) of 0.845 with a standard deviation of 0.0101, comparable to the performance observed with KEGG data. Notably, when focusing on metabolic pathways, the MCC for MetaCyc's predictions reaches 0.800 with a standard deviation of 0.021, representing an improvement of over 5.6% compared to KEGG's metabolic-only pathway predictions.

These findings indicate that the MetaCyc dataset can be effectively utilized at the current state-of-the-art performance level, suggesting that its pathways can be predicted with similar accuracy as those from KEGG. The results underscore the potential of leveraging alternative knowledgebases in machine learning-based pathway annotation and prediction tasks.
label: llm

text:
This study investigates the representation learning of hypergraphs, a type of complex network that can capture intricate relationships among nodes. Unlike traditional networks, hypergraphs enable the modeling of multiple interactions between nodes, rendering them suitable for applications such as social network analysis and recommendation systems. However, existing node embedding methods often neglect the structural properties inherent to hypergraphs, leading to suboptimal performance in downstream tasks.

To address this limitation, we propose HyperS2V, a novel approach that focuses on preserving structural similarity within hypergraphs. Our method involves two key components: first, the definition of hyper-degrees to quantify nodes' structural characteristics; second, the development of multi-scale random walk framework for generating node embeddings. These embeddings are then evaluated using both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating superior performance in terms of interpretability and applicability to downstream tasks compared to existing methods. The results highlight the potential of HyperS2V as a robust tool for hypergraph representation learning and its utility in various machine learning applications.
label: llm

text: In the field of computer vision, single image super-resolution (SISR) is a pivotal task that enables the recovery of high-quality images from their low-resolution counterparts. This technique has far-reaching applications in various domains, including facial recognition, video surveillance, medical imaging, remote sensing, and scene text recognition. While conventional SISR methods often struggle to accurately restore fine details, recent advancements in machine learning-based algorithms have shown promising results, particularly those leveraging residual networks. However, these approaches can be computationally intensive due to their large architecture size. To address this issue, we introduce a novel framework, Multi-Scale Deep Residual Networks (MSDRNs), which incorporates mean-std normalization to ensure uniform statistical scaling of residual branches. Additionally, we employ Bayesian hyperparameter optimization to adaptively tune critical parameters and achieve robust convergence. Our approach combines the evaluation of full-reference metrics (PSNR and SSIM) with no-reference NIQE index to comprehensively assess image quality. The proposed method demonstrates superior performance in terms of both quantitative accuracy and human-visual fidelity, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches such as SwinIR and EDSR. By exploiting high-resolution images for accuracy maximization, MSDRNs offer a more efficient and effective solution for SISR tasks.
label: llm

text:
This study contributes to the development of artificial intelligence (AI) solutions for male fertility diagnostics by introducing a comprehensive collection of expert-labeled image datasets. The datasets comprise three distinct categories, each designed to address specific challenges in automated sperm analysis. The raw dataset consists of over 400 high-resolution images of stained bright-field samples containing multiple sperm cells, while the binary classification set comprises cropped image patches labeled as either sperm or non-sperm. Furthermore, a multiclass dataset categorizes individual sperm cells based on DNA fragmentation level, with annotations ranging from large to no halo size.

The datasets were annotated by five experienced embryologists, with final labels determined through majority agreement. This rigorous annotation process ensures the accuracy and reliability of the data, providing a robust foundation for training and benchmarking machine learning models. The availability of these datasets is open, facilitating reproducibility and enabling further research in reproductive health. By leveraging this collection, researchers can develop and evaluate AI-powered systems for sperm detection, classification, and chromatin integrity assessment, ultimately contributing to improved male fertility diagnostics.
label: llm

text:
This dataset addresses a critical gap in multimodal biomechanical recordings during various locomotor tasks by providing synchronized measurements of electromyographic (EMG), inertial (IMU), and plantar pressure data. A total of 45 adults, comprising 15 individuals with unilateral transtibial amputation and 30 without amputation, participated in the study, completing five standardized tasks: level walking, ramp ascent/descent, and stair ascent/descent. Each participant underwent 50 supervised trials, facilitating a comprehensive understanding of gait adaptation across terrains and populations.

The dataset was collected using wireless EMG and IMU sensors (Delsys Trigno Avanti) to measure muscle activation and kinematics, as well as intelligent insoles (XSENSOR) to capture plantar pressure distribution. The raw data were stored in.hpf and.XSN formats, with processed outputs available in.csv files, organized by task and sensor type along with complete participant metadata.

Key dataset outputs include time-normalized EMG amplitudes, segment kinematics, and pressure maps across various terrains and populations. Technical and experimental validation of the dataset was conducted during data acquisition to ensure its reliability and accuracy. This comprehensive resource enables quantitative analysis of gait adaptation and supports machine learning approaches for locomotion classification, thereby facilitating research in biomechanics, rehabilitation engineering, robotics, and clinical gait studies. The dataset is provided in accessible formats to promote reuse and foster innovative applications in these fields.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the feasibility of implementing continual learning in quantum machine learning systems, which are poised to surpass classical computers on certain tasks. However, these systems often suffer from catastrophic forgetting, a phenomenon where previously learned information is lost when new data is introduced. To address this challenge, we employed an experimental setup involving a superconducting processor and sequentially trained a quantum classifier on three distinct tasks: image classification and quantum state categorization. Our results show that the quantum classifier exhibited significant catastrophic forgetting, with accuracy dropping to 70% after the third task. Building upon the elastic weight consolidation strategy, we successfully adapted the quantum classifier to incrementally retain knowledge across tasks, achieving an average accuracy of 95.1%. Furthermore, our experiments demonstrated that a quantum classifier can outperform its classical counterpart on sequential tasks involving quantum-engineered data, with comparable model complexity. These findings provide a crucial step towards developing adaptable quantum learning systems capable of handling complex, dynamic environments.
label: llm

text: Categorical feature encoding is a crucial step in machine learning pipelines, as it enables numerical inputs for models that often rely on them. However, traditional methods like one-hot encoding can be suboptimal due to increased dimensionality and lack of consideration for inherent relationships between categories. This study investigates 26 categorical encoding techniques across 13 real-world datasets and 7 machine learning algorithms, categorizing the methods based on predictive task type, model performance, and computational efficiency. Our taxonomy provides a framework for selecting encoders that balance these competing factors. Furthermore, we demonstrate how Safe AI metrics can be integrated into encoding pipelines to assess model robustness and fairness, offering complementary insights beyond traditional evaluation metrics. To facilitate further research and practical application, we introduce EncodeHero: a Python tool allowing users to (1) extend our benchmark with their own datasets and (2) select the most suitable encoding method based on their specific needs and constraints. Our findings contribute to a more informed approach to categorical feature encoding in machine learning, enabling researchers and practitioners to develop more accurate and robust models.
label: llm

text:
Characterization of Serum Metabolomic Profiles in Acute Pancreatitis and Development of Bile Acid-Based Predictive Models Using Artificial Intelligence.

Acute pancreatitis (AP) is a complex and potentially life-threatening condition, necessitating the identification of reliable diagnostic biomarkers for early prediction of severe AP (SAP). The current study aimed to investigate serum metabolomic profiles in patients with distinct disease phases and integrate metabolomics with artificial intelligence to construct predictive models. A multicentre observational protocol was conducted, involving comparative metabolomic analysis of 303 metabolites and 461 lipid species in AP patients. Weighted gene coexpression network analysis revealed significant correlations between clinical parameters and specific metabolic clusters, particularly bile acids (BAs) and lipid species. Targeted quantification of 63 BAs was performed within a validation cohort (n = 948), facilitating the derivation of two distinct BA panels using machine learning algorithms. The first panel, comprising nine BAs, demonstrated high diagnostic accuracy for AP, including among individuals with negative conventional enzymatic biomarkers, and effectively discriminated AP from acute cholangitis. A second panel, consisting of 13 BAs, reliably identified patients at elevated risk for SAP progression. These findings validate the translational potential of machine learning-driven metabolic biomarkers in acute abdominal conditions and underscore the clinical utility of BAs as promising diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers in AP.
label: llm

text:
The clinical heterogeneity of prostate cancer (PCa) remains a significant challenge in the field. To address this issue, we employed a multi-omics approach combining single-cell and spatial transcriptomics with explainable machine learning to identify key drivers of lethal tumor behavior. Our analysis of 141,986 high-quality single cells from localized, hormone-sensitive, and castration-resistant PCa revealed a distinct malignant epithelial subpopulation characterized by elevated chromosomal instability, androgen receptor activation, cell-cycle dysregulation, and stemness potential. Spatial mapping further elucidated the presence of immune-enriched yet suppressive niches, where fibroblasts and myeloid cells coexist with exhausted lymphocytes, indicating a functional imbalance in the tumor microenvironment.

Using 101 machine learning pipelines, we identified a Lasso plus PLS-Cox model that demonstrated strong concordance across independent cohorts. The resulting C4-based risk score independently predicted recurrence-free survival after adjustment for age, Gleason score, and T stage. A nomogram combining this score with clinical variables showed good discrimination. SHAP interpretation highlighted MT1M, PCSK1N, and ACSL3 as major risk-driving features. Notably, PCSK1N was found to be progressively upregulated from normal prostate tissue to castration-resistant disease, promoting proliferation, clonogenicity, migration, and enzalutamide resistance. Inhibition of PCSK1N sensitized organoids and xenografts to AR-targeted therapy.

Our findings provide a C4-centered framework for understanding lethal tumor behavior in PCa and offer an experimentally supported approach for prognostic stratification. This study highlights the potential of multi-omics analysis combined with explainable machine learning to identify key drivers of cancer progression and inform targeted therapeutic strategies.
label: llm

text:
The accurate prediction of global natural gas trade is essential for countries to optimize their energy strategies and ensure energy security. Traditional forecasting methods, relying on expert knowledge and statistical techniques, have limitations in capturing the complexity and uncertainty inherent in global natural gas trade. The incorporation of machine learning (ML) approaches has shown promise in modeling these complex factors using historical data. However, existing ML models struggle to account for three unique characteristics of global natural gas trade: network relationships between countries, dynamic changes over time, and influences from numerous complex factors.

To address this gap, a novel hybrid ML model is proposed, combining Graph Attention Networks (GAT), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Transformer architectures. This integrated framework, termed GAT-GT, leverages the strengths of each component: GAT learns annual trade network features, GRU models dynamic relationships, and Transformer combines complex factors with latent features for forecasting. The proposed model benefits from the comprehensive capture of global natural gas trade characteristics, including network, dynamic, and complex-factor influences.

Empirical studies utilizing global natural gas trade data from 2000 to 2023 demonstrate that GAT-GT significantly outperforms eight advanced ML competitors in forecasting accuracy. This research contributes to the development of more effective ML models for predicting global natural gas trade, enabling countries to make informed decisions about their energy strategies and ensuring a more secure energy future.
label: llm

text: The accurate modeling of crystalline materials is crucial for elucidating structure-property relationships in critical materials, thereby accelerating materials discovery. However, recent graph neural networks (GNNs) often struggle to provide physical interpretability and fail to explicitly model the hierarchical and symmetrical nature of crystals. To address this limitation, we propose a novel deep learning framework called Capsule Graph Networks with E(3)-Equivariance (CGN-e3). This framework integrates equivariant message passing with capsule networks to capture both geometric symmetries and hierarchical motif structures. CGN-e3 leverages E(3)-equivariant message passing to learn physically consistent features, which are then organized into capsule representations that can disentangle local motifs and connect them to global properties. We evaluate the effectiveness of our framework on bandgap and formation energy prediction, as well as material classification using the Materials Project and Matbench datasets. Our results demonstrate that CGN-e3 achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.054 eV/atom and 0.379 eV for formation energy and bandgap prediction, respectively, outperforming CGCNN and matching the performance of MEGNet on the same dataset. Furthermore, our framework provides insightful interpretations of the learned capsule representations, offering an unsupervised pathway for interpretable motif discovery. The dynamic routing-by-agreement mechanism identifies and aggregates structurally significant local environments into higher-order graph-level capsules, yielding human-intelligible insights by explicitly quantifying the contribution of specific structural motifs to target material properties.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the classification of healthy controls and patients with obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD) or schizophrenia based on their oculomotor behavior during active visual fixation tasks. Abnormalities in inhibitory control mechanisms, which are essential for cognitive function, have been linked to these neurological disorders. Eye movement studies and machine learning techniques have been employed to differentiate clinical from neurotypical populations. In this research, data from three visual fixation tasks were analyzed using statistical tests to identify saccade features relevant to classification. A shallow Artificial Neural Network (ANN) was implemented for binary and three-class classification. The results demonstrate that selected saccadic features can distinguish between healthy controls and patients with schizophrenia or OCD with moderate accuracy. Notably, the findings suggest that group differences are primarily driven by intrinsic properties of saccadic generation rather than fixation or inhibitory mechanisms. These insights provide preliminary evidence on the potential use of machine learning-based classification methods for distinguishing between neurological disorders based on oculomotor behavior. The study's results contribute to a better understanding of the underlying neurophysiological mechanisms and highlight the need for further research in this area.
label: llm

text:
The accurate detection of depression through speech or conversation has become increasingly important in recent years due to the growing need for effective mental health assessment tools. However, current methods often fall short in terms of effectiveness and interpretability, particularly when dealing with diverse languages and datasets. This study addresses these limitations by proposing a novel model that combines multiple feature extraction and selection techniques to enhance performance.

The proposed method employs an adaptive threshold-based pre-processing technique to eliminate irrelevant information, followed by the application of a twinned Savitzky-Golay filter to minimize noise in the dataset. A Synchro-Squeezed Adaptive Wavelet Transform Algorithm is then used to transform the signal into an image, which is subsequently analyzed using the Singular Empirical Decomposition and Sparse Autoencoder model.

The extracted features are then fused using a Weighted Soft Attention-based Fusion model, before being refined by the Chaotic Mud Ring Optimization algorithm. Finally, a Dilated Convolutional Neural Network based Bidirectional-Long Short Term Memory-Bi-LSTM architecture is utilized to detect different stages of depression, achieving state-of-the-art results.

The proposed method was evaluated on three benchmark datasets: DAIC-WOZ, AVEC 2019, and MELD. The results demonstrate a significant improvement in detection accuracy, with an F1-score of 93.22%, precision of 93.11%, recall of 93.12%, and accuracy of 93.31% on the DAIC-WOZ dataset. Furthermore, the model achieved high accuracy rates of 93.91% and 85.34% on the AVEC 2019 and MELD datasets, respectively. These findings indicate that the proposed method has potential for real-world applications in mental health assessment.
label: llm

text: Recent advancements in Camouflaged Object Detection (COD) have been driven by its diverse practical applications, necessitating the development of more accurate and efficient detection methods. This study presents a novel human-machine collaboration framework for COD, capitalizing on the synergistic benefits of computer vision models and noninvasive brain-computer interfaces. The proposed approach incorporates a multiview backbone to quantify uncertainty in model predictions, utilizing this uncertainty during training to enhance efficiency and accuracy. Furthermore, low-confidence cases are deferred to human evaluation via RSVP-based BCIs during testing, facilitating more reliable decision-making processes. Experimental evaluations on the CAMO dataset demonstrate the efficacy of our framework, achieving state-of-the-art results with significant improvements in balanced accuracy (4.56%) and F1 score (3.66%). Notably, for top-performing participants, enhancements reached 7.6% in balanced accuracy and 6.66% in F1 score. Analysis of training data reveals a strong correlation between confidence and precision, while ablation studies confirm the effectiveness of our training policy and human-machine collaboration strategy. This research contributes to reducing human cognitive load, enhancing system reliability, and establishing a foundation for advancements in real-world COD applications and human-computer interaction.
label: llm

text: Machine learning models have been increasingly employed in predicting acute myeloid leukemia (AML), a clinically aggressive hematologic malignancy driven by complex genetic and epigenetic alterations. Recent studies have highlighted the potential of circular RNAs (circRNAs) as diagnostic biomarkers, but existing predictive models primarily rely on differential expression without considering the impact of higher-order chromatin organization on circRNA formation and function. This study proposes a novel machine learning framework that incorporates three-dimensional (3D) genome architecture to refine circRNA selection for AML prediction. By mapping 9,565 circRNAs onto a reconstructed 3D chromatin model derived from high-throughput chromosome conformation capture data, we identified spatial clustering patterns and biological pathway enrichment among the circRNAs. Our analysis revealed that eighteen pathways exhibited significant 3D aggregation of circRNAs, enabling radial stratification based on nuclear localization. To develop predictive models, five circRNA panels were designed using complementary strategies combining expression, pathway, and spatial features. Cross-validation and external validation across six machine learning algorithms demonstrated that the panel derived from the fifth radial layer (Panel-3DG-Radius5) achieved the most robust and consistent performance, with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve exceeding 0.99. The integration of 3D genomic context reduced feature collinearity while enhancing biological interpretability, establishing a paradigm for circRNA biomarker discovery that leverages spatial genome organization to improve the precision and robustness of AML predictive modeling.
label: llm

text:
Here's a rewritten version of the abstract:

The development of brain-controlled wheelchair (BCW) technology has revolutionized mobility assistance for individuals with motor impairments, enabling direct control via non-invasive brain-computer interfaces (BCIs). Motor imagery-based BCIs (MI-BCIs), which rely on neural activity associated with imagined movements, have gained popularity due to their intuitive and stimulus-free nature. However, achieving reliable multi-class classification accuracy in MI-BCI systems remains a significant challenge. To address this issue, we propose an advanced feature extraction algorithm, EHT-CSP, which integrates Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition Hilbert-Huang Transform (EEMD-HHT) with Time-Frequency Common Spatial Pattern (TFCSP). Our approach extracts marginal spectrum entropy and energy spectrum entropy using EEMD-HHT, followed by the combination of these features with TFCSP-derived feature vectors to enhance classification performance. We employ Light Gradient Boosting Machine for classification and evaluate our proposed MI-BCI system through both offline analysis and real-world BCW obstacle avoidance experiments. Results demonstrate that EHT-CSP achieves an average classification accuracy of 78.45%, with all participants successfully completing BCW navigation tasks, outperforming existing models in both efficacy and robustness.
label: llm

text: In the context of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), where medical devices and real-time monitoring nodes generate highly skewed and burst-like traffic, traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) often struggle to accurately model these patterns. Specifically, Gaussian Naïve Bayes classifiers have been shown to underperform due to their reliance on symmetric feature distributions, which do not hold in IoMT networks. This study addresses this limitation by introducing NBGamma, a modified Naïve Bayes classifier that incorporates a Gamma-based likelihood formulation to better capture positively skewed traffic features. The proposed approach mathematically integrates the Gamma distribution into the likelihood computation and formulates the IDS task as a security-driven optimization problem. Experimental evaluation on the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset demonstrates significant improvements in precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC compared to Gaussian Naïve Bayes under both unbalanced and SMOTE-balanced settings. The results highlight the potential of NBGamma to enhance lightweight IDS modeling for resource-constrained IoMT environments, enabling more effective detection of malicious traffic patterns and advancing security-driven optimization in healthcare networks. Overall, this study contributes to the development of robust and reliable intrusion detection systems for IoMT applications.
label: llm

text: Alzheimer's disease is a debilitating neurodegenerative disorder that affects cognitive function by impairing neurotransmitters, neurons, and tissue in the human brain. Despite the lack of an effective cure, medications can slow disease progression. Early detection of Alzheimer's disease is critical for treatment and further research, but current challenges include limited trained samples and excessive feature descriptions when applying different classification algorithms. Moreover, there is a scarcity of reliable AI-based solutions for detecting this disease. To address these issues, we developed a system utilizing supervised machine learning models to facilitate the initial detection of Alzheimer's disease through a web application. Specifically, we employed five classification algorithms: Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and K-Nearest Neighbors, on a dataset obtained from Kaggle featuring 35 attributes and 2149 data points. To optimize performance, we balanced the dataset and evaluated feature importance. Our results indicate that the Random Forest model achieved the highest accuracy of 91.19%, providing a promising approach for early Alzheimer's disease detection.
label: llm

text: This study assesses the effectiveness of a novel, compact, and lightweight dual-band switched-beam antenna designed for 5G sub-6 GHz communications, operating at 0.7 GHz and 2.6 GHz. The proposed antenna structure incorporates innovative features such as grooves and parasitic elements to enhance bandwidth performance. To optimize seven critical dimensional parameters, three machine learning models – decision tree, random forest, and XGBoost – are employed to facilitate broadband operation. A comprehensive dataset of 244 samples is generated from 305 simulated scenarios created using CST Microwave Studio, simulating a wide range of conditions targeting resonance frequencies and corresponding bandwidths. The incorporation of a short-circuit mechanism enables beam steering in two directions across both frequency bands. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves significant improvements in bandwidth, with enhancements of 11.52 MHz (113.72%) at 0.63 GHz and 128.72 MHz (200.59%) at 2.48 GHz compared to a conventional reference antenna. Empirical measurements validate the simulation outcomes, confirming the antenna's ability to switch beams effectively and operate in wideband dual-band mode. The proposed design's simplicity, compactness, and machine learning-driven performance make it an attractive alternative for next-generation wireless systems.
label: llm

text:
Predictive modeling of Patient Length of Stay (LOS) in resource-limited settings is a complex task, influenced by data skewness, outlier sensitivity, and imbalanced datasets. In such contexts, machine learning (ML) offers potential solutions that require validation through rigorous evaluation. This study aims to develop and assess the effectiveness of ML models for predicting prolonged LOS (≥ 6 days) at Debre Markos Comprehensive Specialized Hospital in Ethiopia, leveraging electronic health records (EHRs) to optimize operational planning.

A retrospective analysis of 27,268 patient records from four hospital wards (2017–2024) was conducted. Data preprocessing involved feature engineering, one-hot encoding, and addressing class imbalance using the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). Eight ML models were trained and evaluated using a range of metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC, with 5-fold cross-validation to ensure robustness. Feature importance was assessed via permutation analysis.

The study found that SMOTE significantly improved model performance, particularly in terms of recall and F1-scores. Gradient Boosting emerged as the top-performing model (AUC: 0.70, Accuracy: 81%, Precision: 72%, Recall: 63%, F1-score: 0.76). Key predictors of prolonged LOS included ward type, age, pregnancy-related conditions, respiratory/digestive diagnoses, and rural residence.

This research demonstrates the potential of ML-driven LOS prediction in resource-constrained settings using EHR data. The findings have implications for optimizing bed management, staffing, and resource allocation, aligning with Ethiopia's Health Sector Transformation Plan (HSTP) goals for efficient healthcare delivery. Future work should focus on real-time validation and integration into hospital systems to maximize the impact of ML-driven LOS prediction.
label: llm

text:
This study addresses a critical challenge in understanding the strengthening mechanisms of high-entropy alloys (HEAs), which have garnered significant attention due to their potential for enhanced mechanical properties. Conventional theories of solid solution strengthening are insufficient to capture the complex interactions between alloy components and dislocations in HEAs. To bridge this knowledge gap, we develop a data-driven framework that leverages large-scale molecular dynamics simulations to investigate the intrinsic strength of face-centered cubic (FCC) HEAs.

Our approach involves computing dislocation mobility under various temperatures and compositions, revealing intricate glide behavior characterized by jerky and wavy motion due to localized pinning effects. By extracting critical resolved shear stress (CRSS) values at 0 K from these simulations, we identify a significant correlation between CRSS and the standard deviation of atomic pinning strength.

Building on this insight, we propose a set of atomic features that capture local structural and compositional fluctuations in HEAs. These features are then used to construct a symbolic model, which predicts the variability of atomic pinning strength using the Sure Independence Screening and Sparsifying Operator method. Our framework provides a unique combination of mechanistic understanding and predictive capabilities, enabling the design of strong, compositionally complex alloys with enhanced mechanical properties.
label: llm

text:
Automated grading of essays has emerged as a crucial component in enhancing the efficiency of English education by mitigating human bias and increasing scalability in assessment. However, conventional rule-based and machine learning methods often fall short due to their inherent limitations. This study proposes a novel hybrid scoring model that integrates Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi LSTM), Transformer encoder, and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (Light GBM) to overcome these weaknesses. The Deep Essays Dataset, comprising diverse student populations with human-annotated scores, is utilized as the basis for this research.

The proposed approach involves a multi-faceted text processing strategy that includes deep preprocessing, shallow and semantic feature extraction, and context representation fusion facilitated by Bi LSTM and Transformer layers. These features are then fed into a Light GBM regressor to predict final scores. Large-scale experiments demonstrate that the hybrid model significantly outperforms existing methods on Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Pearson correlation metrics. Moreover, evaluation metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score consistently exceed 99%, underscoring the stability of the proposed system. Graphical analysis using Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) and Precision-Recall (PR) plots further attest to the robustness of the model.

The findings of this study demonstrate the efficacy of combining deep neural networks and gradient boosting in identifying linguistic and semantic patterns within essays. This innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize automated scoring technology, leading to more equitable, reliable, and scalable assessment in English learning.
label: llm

text:
The development of type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) often goes unnoticed until complications arise, underscoring the need for early detection and prevention through transparent machine learning models. This study aimed to develop and evaluate an interpretable Extra Trees Classifier (ETC) for early T2DM risk prediction within the PERSIAN Dena Cohort, prioritizing probability calibration, fairness, and clinical interpretability.

A dataset comprising 3,203 adults aged 35-70 years was analyzed, with seventy-nine variables initially considered before feature elimination retained fifteen informative predictors. The ETC model was optimized through randomized hyperparameter search and evaluated using ten-fold cross-validation with an internal-external split (80/20). Isotonic regression was employed to calibrate probability estimates, while SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Morris sensitivity analysis were used to examine model transparency and feature influence.

The study found that the ETC exhibited moderate discrimination and stable internal consistency, with cross-validated performance metrics indicating mean accuracy of 0.69 ± 0.03 and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.69 ± 0.04. However, calibration revealed under-confident probability estimates following isotonic regression. Sensitivity analyses identified fasting blood sugar, fatty-liver status, age, kidney-stone history, and triglycerides as dominant predictors, with lifestyle factors exerting secondary influence.

The calibrated ETC provided transparent insights into feature interactions and data limitations, underscoring the importance of interpretability and fairness in trustworthy clinical AI. While predictive power was limited, this study highlights the need for further research to expand predictor diversity, address class imbalance, and validate across other PERSIAN cohorts to develop a more generalizable and interpretable model for early T2DM risk prediction.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of online information has necessitated the development of sophisticated tools for filtering and generating personalized recommendations, thereby enhancing decision-making in the e-commerce sector. Recommender systems (RSs), which analyze individual user preferences, past reviews, and product ratings, have become indispensable components of modern e-commerce platforms. This study undertakes a comprehensive examination of RSs by reviewing research articles published between 2020 and 2025 from various journals and conferences. The review focuses on emerging techniques aimed at improving system performance, including the application of machine learning, deep learning, large language models, and conversational recommender systems. By exploring these cutting-edge methodologies, this study seeks to address significant research gaps and identify key factors influencing RS platforms. A thorough analysis of existing literature and prevailing research challenges provides a foundation for understanding the current state and future direction of RSs in the e-commerce industry. The findings of this study are expected to provide valuable insights into the evolution of RSs and inform the development of more effective recommendation systems.
label: llm

text:
The increasing reliance on artificial intelligence in medical diagnosis has led to a growing interest in leveraging machine learning and deep learning algorithms for classifying pulmonary diseases. Spirometry, a widely used lung function test, plays a crucial role in early disease detection and monitoring. This study investigates the performance of various machine learning and deep learning models in distinguishing between different pulmonary conditions using standardized spirometry features from the Global Lung Initiative 2012 dataset.

To achieve this objective, we employed a range of classification algorithms, including logistic regression, support vector machines, k-nearest neighbors, random forests, XGBoost, as well as deep neural networks and convolutional neural networks. Preprocessing and standardization techniques were applied to enhance model accuracy. The performance of each model was evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1-score, F1-macro, and the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve.

Our results indicate that tree-based models exhibit superior generalization performance on a subject-disjoint test set. Specifically, the XGBoost model achieved an accuracy of 99.24 ± 0.59%, followed by the random forest model at 97.23 ± 2.04%. In contrast, deep learning models, such as deep neural networks and convolutional neural networks, yielded lower accuracy rates of 81.61 ± 5.83% and 77.57 ± 5.55%, respectively.

This study highlights the potential of machine learning-based approaches in early disease diagnosis. However, further optimization is required to improve model performance. The findings have significant implications for integrating artificial intelligence-supported spirometry analysis into clinical practice, underscoring the need for validation on larger patient cohorts to ensure seamless integration into healthcare systems.
label: llm

text: The integration of machine learning and chemistry through molecular representation learning (MRL) has become a pivotal area of research, with significant implications for predicting molecular properties and facilitating complex tasks such as drug discovery. However, existing MRL methods often falter when confronted with out-of-distribution (OOD) data, which is a common occurrence in real-world molecular environments characterized by dynamic and uncertain conditions. To address this limitation, we present EISG, an innovative framework designed to enhance the generalization capability of MRL models on OOD data. By leveraging environmental inference and subgraph generation techniques, EISG aims to capture the invariant properties of molecular graphs across diverse environments. Specifically, our approach involves the development of an unsupervised environmental classification model that identifies latent variables generated by different distributions, as well as a subgraph extractor based on information bottleneck theory that extracts invariant representations from molecular graphs closely related to prediction labels. Through the combination of novel learning objectives and the collaborative work of the environmental classifier and subgraph extractor, EISG enables MRL models to identify invariant graph representations in various environments, thereby achieving more robust OOD generalization. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of our framework across multiple OOD settings, underscoring its potential for improving the reliability and accuracy of molecular representation learning.
label: llm

text: The increasing reliance on Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms in various domains has highlighted the critical importance of high-quality training data. However, the availability of large-scale datasets is often hindered by strict privacy regulations, resulting in reduced dataset sizes and under-representation of certain demographic groups. This data scarcity can be detrimental to AI performance, as it perpetuates existing biases and limitations within the algorithms. To mitigate this issue, researchers have explored synthetic data generation techniques that create artificial samples with similar properties to real-world data. This study examines the current state of synthetic tabular data generation methods, focusing on statistical, probabilistic, and Machine Learning-based approaches. A comprehensive analysis of high-quality peer-reviewed articles reveals that synthetic data not only increases dataset size but also enhances its quality by promoting diversity and representation of various demographic groups. Notably, techniques such as Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) and its variants have demonstrated significant performance improvements in classification tasks, with reported gains up to 12%. However, the efficacy of synthetic data generation ultimately depends on the underlying process, characteristics of real-world data, and evaluation metrics used to assess quality.
label: llm

text: The metabolic reprogramming of cancer cells is characterized by altered lactate accumulation, but its spatial and cell-type-specific effects in lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) remain poorly understood. This study integrates single-cell transcriptomics, spatial transcriptomics, spatial metabolomics, and immunofluorescence with large-scale clinical data to elucidate the role of lactate in shaping the tumor microenvironment. By analyzing high-lactate tumors, we observe increased epithelial and fibroblast populations, whereas low-lactate samples exhibit enriched T/NK cells and monocytes/macrophages. Spatial metabolomics reveals cell-type-specific lactate and pyruvate distributions, with endothelial cells showing minimal lactate accumulation. Notably, endothelial subclusters in high-lactate tissues display angiogenic and stress-response signatures, which are strongly associated with poor prognosis. Machine-learning models consistently identify endothelial and fibroblast programs as key determinants of high-lactate states and adverse clinical outcomes. Our multi-omics spatial profiling demonstrates that lactate plays a pivotal role in driving angiogenesis, immune suppression, and prognostic stratification in LUAD, highlighting lactate-centered pathways as promising therapeutic targets for improving patient outcomes.
label: llm

text: The increasing prevalence of herbal medicines and natural dietary supplements has heightened concerns about herb-drug interactions (HDIs) in modern pharmacotherapy. HDIs can significantly impact drug efficacy or safety by altering pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic processes. However, the complexity and cost of experimental validation have hindered comprehensive understanding of these interactions. To address this knowledge gap, we present a novel graph-based computational framework for predicting the effects of HDIs. Our approach constructs a heterogeneous, multi-relational graph incorporating drug-drug interactions (DDIs), drug-target interactions (DTIs), and herb-target interactions (HTIs). By employing metapath-guided random walks to generate node embeddings, we capture the intricate relationships within this complex graph. These embeddings are then integrated with molecular fingerprints, tabular descriptors, and target node encodings to train various supervised classifiers. Our extensive experimental evaluation demonstrates the efficacy of this approach, particularly with tree-based models such as XGBoost and Random Forest, which achieve high predictive performance in multi-class classification tasks. The results underscore the potential of AI-powered graph-based methodologies to support integrative and precision medicine by facilitating scalable and accurate prediction of herb-drug interaction effects.
label: llm

text: This study addresses the pressing need for accurate stress detection systems by developing a machine learning-based framework that leverages two large-scale multimodal physiological datasets: NEURO and WESAD. The proposed approach utilizes both classical machine learning models and deep learning architectures to classify different types of stress. To effectively extract relevant features from time-series data, raw signals are converted into image representations using novel signal transformation techniques, including Gramian Angular Summation Fields (GAFs), Gramian Angular Difference Fields (GAFd), and Markov Transition Fields (MTF). Unlike conventional methods that focus on temporal transformations, our approach performs feature-axis transformations to capture spatial patterns in physiological signals. The Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is employed to extract spatial features from these visual representations, significantly enhancing classification performance. Furthermore, Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) visualizations identify the most influential input regions driving model decisions, providing insights into the physiological indicators of stress. Our results demonstrate strong generalization across both datasets, with feature-axis transformations based deep learning models achieving 98.3% accuracy and classical models like XGBoost and LightGBM reaching approximately 99% accuracy. The integration of Grad-CAM strengthens the model's interpretability by validating the relevance of learned features. Overall, this study showcases the potential of integrating advanced signal transformation techniques with explainable deep learning to develop robust and interpretable stress detection systems.
label: llm

text: Abdominal organ segmentation in medical images remains a formidable challenge due to the intricate anatomy and low contrast present in these images. To overcome this, we introduce MSAE-U-Net, a novel deep learning architecture designed for accurate and robust abdominal segmentation. This model incorporates three pivotal innovations: the Multi-Scale Deformable Convolutional Block Attention Module (MS-D-CBAM), which facilitates multi-scale attention; the Adaptive Global Context Refinement ASPP (AGCR-ASPP), which dynamically refines context information; and the Rectangular Self-Calibration Module (RCM), which recalibrates spatial features for enhanced segmentation. These modules synergistically enhance both local detail and global context, leading to improved segmentation performance. Evaluations conducted on the Dresden Surgical Anatomy dataset yielded outstanding results for MSAE-U-Net, with a Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 93.13%, IoU of 92.01%, and precision of 94.67%. Notably, these outcomes surpass those of existing models such as U-Net, nnU-Net, and AbdomenNet. The superior performance of MSAE-U-Net underscores its potential for clinical applications, including preoperative planning and automated organ identification.
label: llm

text: The diagnosis of brain tumors remains a critical challenge in oncology, with timely intervention crucial for improved patient outcomes. Recent advancements in medical imaging have led to the development of computer-aided diagnostic systems that leverage image classification techniques. This study proposes and evaluates five hybrid models for multi-class classification of brain tumors, combining the strengths of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with Support Vector Machines (SVM). Specifically, we integrate CNN architectures (VGG16, VGG19, ResNet50, ResNet152, and EfficientNetB3) with LSTM layers to capture temporal dependencies in imaging data. The resulting outputs are then classified using SVM, which provides robust classification capabilities. Our methodology includes data augmentation to address class imbalance and increase the diversity of the training dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed models outperform current state-of-the-art approaches, with the ResNet152 + LSTM + SVM model achieving exceptional performance metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score) when optimized using the RMSprop optimizer. These findings have significant implications for the development of accurate and efficient computer-aided diagnostic systems for brain tumor classification.
label: llm

text:
The convergence of telecommunications and computer vision has revolutionized numerous applications in recent years. However, these two fields have traditionally operated independently, neglecting the potential synergies between their respective innovations. In challenging environments, such as those characterized by obstructions or limited lighting, integrating radio sensing and computer vision can significantly enhance problem-solving capabilities. Machine learning algorithms play a crucial role in fusing heterogeneous data from multiple sources, enabling the extraction of additional knowledge from raw sensor inputs.

This study introduces the CONVERGE paradigm, which seeks to integrate telecommunications and computer vision to create a unified framework for "View-to-Communicate, Communicate-to-View" applications. The proposed architecture is designed to support various use cases, including those in telecommunications, automotive, manufacturing, media, and healthcare. To facilitate research in these areas, CONVERGE provides an open experimental infrastructure that combines wireless communications and computer vision capabilities with a novel set of tools for data collection and analysis.

The results of this study demonstrate the feasibility of integrating telecommunications and computer vision using machine learning algorithms. The experimental infrastructure developed as part of this work enables the creation of large-scale datasets, which can be used to train and evaluate models in various application domains. This research has significant implications for the development of next-generation applications that require the fusion of multiple data sources and modalities.
label: llm

text: This study integrates large-scale genetic and multi-omics datasets to elucidate the host's response to coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), influenza, and respiratory syncytial virus (RSV). By leveraging the COVID-19 Host Genetics Initiative (HGI) Genome-Wide Association Study (GWAS) data and publicly available transcriptomes and plasma proteomics, we construct an explainable artificial intelligence model that compares host infection mechanisms across these viral illnesses. Our analysis reveals shared antiviral pathways, such as type I interferon signaling, which are active in responses to all three viruses, alongside virus-specific mechanisms that distinguish COVID-19 from influenza and RSV. For instance, SARS-CoV-2 induces strong coagulation and renin-angiotensin system dysregulation, whereas influenza triggers robust T-cell activation, and RSV elicits an excessive neutrophil-driven inflammatory response. Genetic risk pathway fingerprints from GWAS highlight associations between COVID-19 severity and variants in interferon and inflammatory pathways, while host genetic effects in influenza point to distinct receptor usage with minimal overlap. Mendelian randomization identifies key causal proteins for COVID-19 severity, including ABO (blood group glycosyltransferase) and inflammatory mediators, suggesting that host glycomic and immune factors modulate disease outcomes. Our model integrates these multi-omic features to accurately distinguish COVID-19 from other viral infections, with SHAP interpretation confirming the predominance of the identified mechanisms in model predictions. This study provides a comprehensive comparative map of host responses in COVID-19 versus influenza and RSV, yielding biologically interpretable insights into both common antiviral defenses and unique pathogenic pathways that inform targeted therapies for severe respiratory viral diseases.
label: llm

text: This study addresses critical challenges in 3D human pose estimation (HPE) and energy expenditure estimation (EEE), focusing on handling complex activities, improving generalization, and jointly enhancing both tasks within a unified framework. To address these challenges, we propose Pose2Met, an end-to-end framework that integrates HPE and EEE through a novel SpatioTemporal Aggregated Pose (STAP) representation. This representation is the core component of STAPFormer, a Transformer model designed for efficient and accurate motion modeling. By leveraging this representation, Pose2Met introduces a unified learning strategy that jointly optimizes pose dynamics and metabolic patterns within a single paradigm, enabling direct prediction of both 3D pose and energy expenditure from 2D pose inputs. Our results demonstrate the efficacy of Pose2Met in achieving performance comparable to traditional pipelines while significantly enhancing computational efficiency and robustness in practical applications. Experiments on Human3.6M and Vid2Burn-ADL datasets show that STAPFormer outperforms state-of-the-art models, with an MPJPE of 38.2 mm and a 22.1 kcal MAE for EEE. Under the unified learning framework, 2D pose-based EEE approaches the accuracy of 3D pose-based prediction, highlighting the potential of Pose2Met in intelligent fitness and healthcare applications.
label: llm

text: This study addresses the challenges of sentiment analysis in short-form, noisy social media messages, where linguistic irregularities such as slang, emojis, and misspellings compromise model performance and interpretability. To mitigate these issues, a novel hybrid model is proposed that integrates a convolutional block with a bidirectional long short-term memory network (BiLSTM) and a multi-head self-attention mechanism incorporating a noise-invariant contrastive head (NICH). This module normalizes noisy tokens before encoding, enabling the model to focus on sentiment-bearing tokens. The proposed approach is trained in two stages: an initial frozen phase followed by fine-tuning of word embeddings. Evaluation is conducted on two benchmark datasets: Sentiment140 (1.6M tweets) and IMDb (50k movie reviews). Results demonstrate that the hybrid model outperforms classical baselines, including logistic regression, support vector machines, and random forests, as well as an Attention-BiLSTM reference model. Notably, it achieves competitive performance with a compact Transformer-based baseline (DistilBERT), attaining 90.63% accuracy and 0.9079 F1 on Sentiment140 and 91.90% accuracy and 0.9203 F1 on IMDb. Furthermore, attention scores and a perturbation-based metric reveal that the model consistently focuses on sentiment-carrying tokens, providing valuable token-level explanations for real-time monitoring of customer feedback and brand perception in noisy social media streams.
label: llm

text: Financial technology (Fin-tech) startups play a crucial role in driving innovation and disruption within the financial sector; however, their high failure rates underscore the need for sophisticated predictive tools to inform investment and policy decisions. To address this challenge, we develop a deep learning framework based on 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to categorize Fin-tech startup outcomes into four distinct categories: Initial Public Offering (IPO), Merger & Acquisition (M&A), Closure, and Unicorn status using a comprehensive dataset from Crunchbase. By leveraging the ability of CNNs to extract local and hierarchical patterns from structured scalar attributes such as team composition, funding velocity, investor diversity, and company age, our framework aims to improve predictive accuracy and mitigate class imbalance through the application of ADASYN oversampling. Our results demonstrate that XGBoost achieves state-of-the-art performance on this task, with 92.9% accuracy and 91.9% macro F1, while the proposed CNN model achieves a competitive 90.8% accuracy and 89.6% macro F1. Notably, our study highlights the trade-offs between tree-based models and deep learning approaches in multiclass startup outcome prediction, with implications for both research and practice in Fin-tech innovation and policy-making.
label: llm

text: In the context of smart agricultural IoT systems, dynamic resource allocation poses significant challenges due to device heterogeneity and environmental variability. To address these issues, we present a novel framework that integrates edge computing capabilities from emerging 6G networks with reinforcement learning techniques for optimizing resource management. By leveraging the ultra-low latency and high bandwidth of 6G, our framework enables real-time communication and efficient task offloading, while reinforcement learning dynamically adapts to changing environment states and task requirements. A bespoke reward function is designed to balance competing objectives, including latency reduction, resource utilization, and energy efficiency. Experimental evaluations demonstrate substantial performance improvements, with a notable 35% decrease in latency and 25% increase in resource utilization compared to traditional approaches. Furthermore, our framework exhibits scalability under increasing device density and task complexity, underscoring its potential for applications such as precision irrigation, autonomous agriculture, and drone surveillance in smart agricultural settings.
label: llm

Here is a rewritten abstract that meets the requirements:

The increasing availability of large datasets and advancements in artificial intelligence have led to the widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) across various fields, including healthcare. The efficient utilization of extensive patient data and medical records has been shown to enhance diagnostic accuracy and enable disease-risk prediction. However, a comprehensive evaluation framework for assessing the application of LLMs in structured and unstructured medical data is lacking. This study proposes the HLLM-Potential Framework (Healthcare Large Language Model Potential Evaluation Framework), a systematic approach designed to evaluate the applicability and performance of LLMs in medical data analysis through comparative experiments with traditional models. Our methodology involves training existing LLMs on publicly available cardiovascular datasets, which encompass both structured and unstructured data, to perform disease prediction and health-risk assessment tasks. We also compare the predictive performance of various LLMs with traditional machine-learning and deep-learning models to quantify their differences. The results demonstrate that LLMs can effectively process structured and unstructured medical data, achieving accuracy and recall rates above 80% in cardiovascular disease prediction tasks. Furthermore, our experiments show that multimodal LLMs exhibit superior performance in ECG image classification tasks, with an overall accuracy rate of 85%. These findings suggest that LLMs offer notable advantages over traditional models, including high efficiency, flexibility, and multimodal integration capabilities, making them a promising tool for healthcare applications.
label: llm

text: Sparse signal recovery is a fundamental problem in various fields, including medical imaging, remote sensing, machine learning, and data science. Many practical applications involve signals that are inherently non-negative due to the physical nature of measured quantities (e.g., pixel intensities, chemical concentrations, or power spectra). While there has been significant research on general sparse recovery and a growing body of work on non-negative sparse recovery, existing methods often treat these cases separately rather than providing a unified framework. This study proposes a novel sequential quadratic programming (SQP) framework that treats both general and non-negative sparse recovery as special instances of a single constrained optimization model, distinguished by the geometry of the trust region used to enforce feasibility. The framework leverages the Smoothed-ℓ0 approximation, which provides a smooth surrogate for the combinatorial ℓ0 norm. By exploiting the exact diagonal structure of the Hessian of the SL0 objective, we achieve accurate second-order search directions and superlinear convergence rates, outperforming first-order methods prevalent in the literature. For non-negative recovery, we incorporate interior-point ideas directly into the SQP subproblem, imposing an ellipsoidal trust region that keeps iterates strictly inside the non-negative orthant, ensuring stability and theoretical soundness. Numerical experiments on synthetic data and real-world tasks demonstrate that our unified framework outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms in terms of recovery accuracy, phase transition behavior, and robustness to noise.
label: llm

text: Estimating heterogeneous treatment effects (HTE) is a crucial task in many fields, particularly in healthcare and economics, where understanding the impact of interventions on diverse populations is essential for informed decision-making. However, existing methods often struggle with confounding variables, leading to biased estimates of treatment effects. To address this limitation, we introduce Bayesian causal forests with covariate balancing propensity scores (BCF-CBPS), a novel approach that integrates covariate balancing directly into the propensity score estimation step. By leveraging the strengths of both BCF and covariate balancing techniques, our method improves covariate balance and enables more accurate estimation of true treatment effect heterogeneity. Through extensive Monte Carlo simulations and real-world applications, we demonstrate that BCF-CBPS outperforms standard BCF and other machine learning methods for HTE estimation in terms of precision and robustness. The results highlight the potential of combining covariate balancing with the BCF model to provide reliable estimates of treatment effects in complex observational studies.
label: llm

text: This study introduces a novel machine learning framework for the automated analysis and clustering of modern and contemporary Korean paintings based on image data. By integrating a pre-trained vision-language architecture with multi-layered feature extraction, our approach efficiently captures detailed formal characteristics, including color features from multiple color spaces and quantified texture information. The extracted feature vectors are then clustered using majority-label assignment, yielding an overall accuracy of 82.4%. This surpasses the performance of single-feature baselines (RGB: 82.0%, HSV: 81.3%, histogram: 51.0%, LBP: 68.8%, and GLCM: 73.7%). Notably, our method exhibits improved per-artist precision, enhanced boundary-case discrimination, and increased robustness for low-sample categories. The resulting artist clusters are characterized by unique color and texture profiles, as exemplified by representative images. Furthermore, we extend the analysis through automatic image captioning and zero-shot style assignment via matching image and text embeddings. Our findings demonstrate that machine learning-based image analysis offers a reliable and objective methodology for identifying and distinguishing visual characteristics of modern and contemporary Korean paintings, providing a quantitative approach to art-historical interpretation.
label: llm

text:
Landslide risk identification is a critical component of disaster relief operations and sustainable land use planning, as it enables the accurate mapping of susceptibility zones. However, traditional detection techniques often fall short due to their high workload, subjectivity, and inaccuracy, particularly when dealing with inaccessible landslide distributions. To address these limitations, this study proposes an explainable machine learning approach for landslide risk identification that integrates knowledge about probable landslide-causing factors.

The proposed method is based on a multistage fusion architecture, which improves the identification of different possible landslide types by selecting the most relevant features through an information gain filter-based feature selection technique. This is followed by training several linear and ensemble classification models using the filtered features. The resulting ensemble model demonstrates superior performance in landslide prediction tasks compared to other machine learning models. Furthermore, the proposed approach utilizes effective visualization techniques for accurate explainability, providing insights into the decision-making process.

The results of this study demonstrate the potential of the proposed method to improve landslide risk identification and provide valuable information for disaster relief operations and sustainable land use planning. The use of an explainable machine learning strategy addresses the limitations of traditional detection techniques, enabling more accurate and reliable landslide susceptibility mapping.
label: llm

text:
The proliferation of social networks has led to an unprecedented surge in text mining applications, with text analysis emerging as a pivotal technique for extracting valuable insights from online content. Social media platforms offer unparalleled access to vast amounts of text data, comprising user-generated comments that hold significant business value when analyzed. Sentiment analysis (SA), a subfield of natural language processing, aims to automatically identify and categorize opinions expressed in text as positive, negative, or neutral. This capability is particularly valuable for understanding public opinion, especially when applied to large-scale textual data from social media platforms.

However, the extraction of high-quality datasets from social media platforms is often hindered by platform-specific APIs, privacy constraints, and data usage limitations. To address this challenge, we propose TSAPM-BERT, a BERT-based framework that integrates a weighted attention mechanism with sentiment lexicon information and optimizes the learning rate to enhance aspect-level sentiment classification. We evaluate the performance of TSAPM-BERT on a benchmark emotion dataset, comparing its results against traditional machine learning and deep learning baselines.

Experimental results demonstrate that TSAPM-BERT achieves a training accuracy of 99.37% and testing accuracy of 98.52%, outperforming competing methods. This study provides a reproducible framework that bridges the gap between high-accuracy sentiment classification and the practical constraints of social media data usage, offering a valuable contribution to the field of text analysis and sentiment analysis.
label: llm

text:
This study presents a comprehensive image dataset designed for surface defect detection on waterborne painted wood products. The dataset comprises 13,400 high-resolution images, encompassing four distinct defect types: scratches, cracks, bubbles, and holes. Specifically, the dataset includes 3,645 bubble defects, 3,498 scratch defects, 3,256 crack defects, and 3,001 hole defects. These images were captured in an operational production facility in Jiangshan, China, utilizing specialized industrial cameras. To ensure data accuracy, professional annotators performed rigorous labeling of the images.

The primary objective of this research is to provide a critical resource for deploying deep learning models in real-world industrial assembly lines. By leveraging this dataset, researchers can develop and evaluate automated machine learning solutions for multi-class defect detection. Such techniques have the potential to significantly enhance defect detection and remediation processes, thereby ensuring finish integrity and final product quality. The dataset's release is expected to facilitate advancements in surface defect detection, ultimately contributing to improved manufacturing efficiency and product reliability.
label: llm

text: Concept drift is a pervasive issue in machine learning-based systems, compromising the integrity and reliability of pre-trained models' predictions.
