To mitigate this challenge, effective drift detection and adaptation mechanisms are essential components in operational systems. Recent research has focused on deep learning-based methods, particularly autoencoders, due to their versatility in handling various tasks. Autoencoders learn data distributions and measure deviations using reconstruction loss; however, standard autoencoders may not always achieve optimal performance. This study investigates the optimization of autoencoder-based drift detection methods by exploring different types of autoencoders, including denoising, variational, and standard variants. A framework is proposed to select the best hyperparameters and parameters for each method using grid search and Bayesian optimization (BO). The framework is evaluated theoretically and empirically on eight datasets, comprising four synthetic and four real-world datasets. Results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in identifying optimal autoencoder configurations with improved drift detection performance, underscoring its potential to enhance the robustness of machine learning-based systems in dynamic environments.
label: llm

text: This study addresses the challenge of utilizing social media data for urban and regional studies or public health monitoring by developing a classification framework to infer tweet locations with high precision. Given that only a small fraction (2%) of Twitter posts are geo-annotated, our approach relies solely on textual and contextual information within each post to predict its location. The proposed method offers worldwide coverage and adapts its spatial granularity according to the volume of posts in a given area, allowing for country-level predictions in low-activity regions and state-level predictions in high-activity zones. To evaluate the framework's performance, we conduct extensive experiments examining the impact of various factors on different regions around the world. Our analysis reveals that excluding tweets with limited location hints significantly enhances overall model quality, suggesting a trade-off between data inclusion and prediction accuracy.
label: llm

text: The scarcity of data in certain engineering applications has been a long-standing challenge for machine learning methods. In tasks such as predictive maintenance, where faults are rare and expensive to obtain, the value of data is akin to gold. While synthetic data generation has gained significant attention in fields like computer vision and natural language processing, its application in time series analysis remains underexplored. Recent advancements in generative models have shown promise in augmenting time series data, but the use of denoising techniques, particularly diffusion probabilistic models (DPMs), warrants further investigation. This study aims to address this knowledge gap by developing and evaluating a novel methodology for data augmentation in time series classification and regression tasks. The proposed D3A-TS framework leverages DPMs to generate synthetic data, which is conditioned on meta-attributes to enhance its realism. An extensive evaluation using six diverse datasets demonstrates the efficacy of this approach in creating high-quality training data. Furthermore, an ablation study provides insight into the contribution of individual components to the overall performance. The results highlight the potential of D3A-TS as a valuable tool for augmenting time series data and improving the accuracy of machine learning models.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the integration of machine learning and natural language processing techniques in film and television script creation, with a focus on script generation, plot construction, and character development. A deep neural network-based model is developed to generate scripts by leveraging a large dataset of existing film and television scripts. The model's performance is evaluated in terms of fluency, accuracy, and creativity, demonstrating promising results in short script generation. However, the model exhibits limitations in handling complex plots and nuanced emotional expressions, highlighting areas for future improvement. To address these challenges, ongoing research aims to enhance the model's capacity for intricate plot processing and emotional expression, while also optimizing computational efficiency and output quality. The findings of this study provide a foundational empirical basis for exploring the application of artificial intelligence in creative industries, offering new avenues for technological innovation and practical exploration in script creation.
label: llm

text: This study addresses the pressing need for high-resolution soil moisture data at various depth horizons to improve our understanding of the Earth's energy and water cycles. Current products primarily focus on surface layers, leaving a significant gap in continuous datasets for deep soil horizons. To bridge this knowledge gap, we developed a global, daily multilayer soil moisture dataset (SWSM) spanning 2002-2021 using an advanced machine learning approach based on the XGBoost algorithm. The SWSM dataset provides spatially explicit estimates at a resolution of 0.05° for three distinct depth layers: 0-10 cm, 10-30 cm, and 30-60 cm. Through rigorous validation against in situ observations, our results demonstrate exceptional accuracy, with Pearson correlation coefficients surpassing 0.90 and root mean square errors below 0.05 across all depths. Furthermore, a feature importance analysis confirms the dataset's physical consistency, revealing depth-dependent patterns that align with established hydrological principles. The SWSM dataset offers valuable insights for various applications, including hydrologic modeling, agricultural water management, and climate change research, thanks to its comprehensive temporal coverage, fine spatial resolution, and multi-layer structure.
label: llm

text: This study introduces ML-TWiX, a comprehensive dataset of monthly total water storage anomalies (TWSA) spanning from 1980 to 2012 on a global grid with a spatial resolution of 0.5° × 0.5°. The existing GRACE and GRACE Follow-On satellite missions have significantly advanced our understanding of TWSA, but their combined record is limited to approximately two decades, hindering long-term climate and hydrological research. To address this limitation, ML-TWiX leverages an ensemble of three machine learning models – Random Forest, XGBoost, and Gaussian Process Regression – trained on a diverse set of global hydrological and land surface model simulations. Each model was applied independently to reconstruct TWSA, and their outputs were subsequently combined through ensemble averaging, yielding a unified product with spatially explicit uncertainty estimates. The accuracy of ML-TWiX is validated against multiple independent datasets, including satellite laser ranging, storage deduced from the water mass balance closure, and global mean sea level budget estimates. This continuous reconstruction of global TWSA facilitates a wide range of applications in hydrology, climate science, and water resource assessment, thereby enhancing our capacity to investigate long-term climate variability and its impacts on global water resources.
label: llm

text:
The disparity between the skills imparted in educational institutions and those demanded by industry has become a pressing concern in today's fast-paced technological landscape. As industries continually evolve, they necessitate workers with up-to-date knowledge and novel competencies. This research endeavors to develop an adaptive framework that leverages machine learning to synchronize industry skill requirements with educational curricula, thereby generating personalized course recommendations.

To address this challenge, the proposed framework aggregates data from multiple sources, including job portals, industry reports, company career pages, and labor market analyses, to capture the current and emerging skill demands across various sectors. The developed Dynamic Termite Life Cycle Optimizer-driven Euclidean-Support Vector Machine (DTLC-Euclidean SVM) model predicts both present and future skill requirements in the job market by analyzing patterns in graduate skillsets and employer needs.

The framework's dynamic matching algorithm employs similarity metrics and k-Means Clustering techniques to map industry demands onto existing course content, while an automated course generation module suggests new or updated courses to address identified skill shortages. The framework's performance is evaluated using standard metrics, yielding accuracy (96.8%), precision (97.6%), recall (96.2%), and f1-score (97.3%) results that underscore its robustness and reliability in bridging the skill gap.

This data-driven approach enables continuous curriculum adaptation, fostering stronger alignment between academia and industry. Ultimately, the framework supports educators and policymakers in developing targeted educational programs that prepare students for real-world career opportunities, enhancing employability and addressing the demands of a rapidly evolving labor market.
label: llm

text: The evaluation of Physical Education (PE) teaching effectiveness is a critical aspect of promoting students' physical development, engagement, and lifelong health performances. However, conventional assessment methods often suffer from subjectivity and fail to capture the complexities of PE instruction. This study aims to develop an integrated framework for evaluating PE teaching effectiveness using Multimodal Machine Learning (MML). A comprehensive dataset was compiled, incorporating instructional delivery (video/audio), physical movement patterns (via wearable sensors and motion capture), and student perceptions. To ensure data quality, two preprocessing techniques were employed: wavelet denoising to clean sensor signals and z-score normalization to control data variability across modalities. The Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) was utilized for feature extraction from video sources, capturing fine-grained movements and posture characteristics. A hybrid assessment framework, the Enhanced Marine Predators-driven Explainable Boosting Machine (EMP-EBM), was developed by combining the EMP's global optimization capabilities with the EBM's transparent structure. The proposed model demonstrated exceptional performance in identifying efficient PE teaching sessions, achieving accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of 98.07%, 97.85%, 98.31%, and 98.07%, respectively. This research contributes to the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education by providing an objective, adaptive, and multifaceted assessment framework for PE teaching effectiveness.
label: llm

text: The development of reliable non-contact vital sign monitoring technologies for multiple patients remains a pressing challenge in healthcare settings. To address this issue, this study introduces a comprehensive radar-based dataset designed to facilitate the evaluation and improvement of simultaneous multi-patient vital sign monitoring methods. In collaboration with Ostrava University Training Hospital, we have created a dataset comprising recordings from two to three advanced medical simulation mannequins that mimic a broad spectrum of physiological states, including healthy resting conditions and acute emergencies such as apnea, cardiac arrest, and severe respiratory distress. The dataset encompasses over 100 uniquely configured sessions, with varied sensor geometries (top, frontal, lateral views at distances of 1-4 meters, and angles of 0°/45°) and radar parameters (ADC samples, chirp loops, ramp times, frame rates, and gains). Preliminary analysis using beamforming-based processing techniques has demonstrated the dataset's potential for developing advanced signal processing and machine learning pipelines. The results show mean heart-rate and breathing-rate errors near clinical thresholds (MAE ≈ 6.6 bpm for HR, 1.47 bpm for RR), underscoring the utility of this dataset in advancing non-invasive multi-patient monitoring systems. In accordance with FAIR principles, all data are thoroughly documented and publicly accessible, supporting reproducible research efforts toward the development of reliable non-contact vital sign monitoring technologies.
label: llm

text: In elderly cardiac surgery patients, intraoperative acquired pressure injury (IAPI) poses a significant risk due to compromised skin integrity and impaired perfusion. Reliable prediction of IAPI risk is crucial for prevention and personalized care. To address this challenge, we developed machine learning models using data from 12,222 patients aged 60 years or older in the MIMIC-IV database. Our approach involved training seven classifiers on 24 clinically relevant features and integrating them via a stacking ensemble method. Internal evaluation employed stratified fivefold cross-validation with 10 repetitions and bootstrapped confidence intervals (2,000 resamples) to quantify uncertainty from missing data. Multiple imputations (m = 2000) were used to account for missing values. External validation was performed on an independent dataset of 355 patients from Nanjing First Hospital, with class imbalance addressed through resampling. Calibration metrics and SHAP analysis with bootstrapping were employed to assess model reliability and interpretability. The ensemble model demonstrated strong discrimination in both internal (AUC = 0.857) and external (AUC = 0.783) validation, exhibiting stable performance across imputations (mean AUC: 0.997 ± 0.004). Calibration analysis confirmed good agreement between predicted and observed risks. Key predictors, including atrial fibrillation, lymphocyte count, and operative time, were clinically plausible and robustly identified. This study presents an externally validated ensemble learning framework for early IAPI risk prediction in elderly cardiac surgery patients, offering improved methodological transparency and a clinically actionable tool for perioperative risk stratification and prevention.
label: llm

text: The escalating prevalence of diabetes worldwide necessitates the development of accurate and timely predictive models to mitigate its severe consequences. Machine learning regression techniques have revolutionized diabetes prediction by elucidating intricate relationships between risk factors and disease progression, thereby enabling more precise and personalized interventions. Recent advancements in feature selection, regularization, and ensemble methods have significantly enhanced the predictive performance of these algorithms. However, the proliferation of diverse datasets, evaluation metrics, and regression techniques has created a pressing need for standardized frameworks to ensure fair and reproducible comparisons. This study aims to address this challenge by conducting an in-depth analysis of current trends in diabetes prediction using regression models. Specifically, we investigate the evolution and performance of machine learning techniques, develop a reference framework for evaluating and benchmarking diabetes prediction models, assess the effectiveness of leading regression algorithms, and outline future research directions that prioritize robust, explainable, and patient-centric prediction models capable of seamless integration into clinical practice.
label: llm

text: Alzheimer's Disease (AD), the leading cause of dementia, necessitates early diagnosis to facilitate effective intervention. To address this critical need, we propose a hybrid framework that combines SqueezeNet, a lightweight convolutional neural network, with ensemble stacking of machine learning models for enhanced AD detection using MRI scans. Leveraging a large dataset comprising 3,382 images across four classes (Mild, Moderate, Very Mild Dementia, and Non-Dementia), our approach extracts efficient features from SqueezeNet while minimizing computational demands without compromising diagnostic accuracy. A stacked ensemble is employed to integrate predictions from diverse models, including Multi-Layer Perceptrons with varying architectures, XGBoost, Cat Boost, and AdaBoost, thereby optimizing robustness and addressing class imbalance through rigorous validation. Our proposed model achieves state-of-the-art performance, boasting an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 94.9% and accuracy of 89%, outperforming individual models and demonstrating superior reliability in clinical environments with limited resources. The framework's scalability, reliability, and cost-effectiveness make it a promising tool for real-time AD detection and classification in clinical settings.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of high-dimensional feature spaces in malware classification has hindered the performance of machine learning models. To mitigate this issue, we investigate the efficacy of four dimensionality-reduction techniques – Principal Component Analysis (PCA), Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection (UMAP), and Autoencoder-based reduction – applied to opcode-frequency representations of malware. Our study utilizes a comprehensive dataset comprising 82,569 samples and 1796 opcodes, allowing us to examine the impact of each reduction method across multiple target dimensions and two classifier architectures: Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and a three-layer Multilayer Perceptron (MLP). The results indicate that LDA achieves superior separability at lower dimensions, while PCA excels at higher dimensions where variance preservation is crucial. In contrast, Autoencoder-based reduction consistently yields high accuracy with compact representations, whereas UMAP exhibits limited benefits for tabular opcode data. Our findings underscore the trade-offs between linear and non-linear reduction strategies, providing valuable insights for selecting efficient feature compression methods in large-scale malware analysis.
label: llm

text: This study addresses the challenge of estimating twin zygosity using self-reported physical similarity features as a cost-effective alternative to genetic testing. A machine learning framework was developed and trained on data from the Iranian School-aged Twin Registry (2018-2021), comprising 5,077 twin pairs with 11 physical similarity traits. To mitigate class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed. Eight machine learning algorithms were evaluated, including K-nearest neighbors, support vector machine, logistic regression, random forest, decision tree, and boosting techniques such as XGBoost and Gradient Boosting Classifier. The performance of each model was assessed using multiple metrics after hyperparameter optimization via GridSearchCV. Results indicate that XGBoost achieved superior accuracy (85.57%) and F1-score (86.72%), outperforming other algorithms. SHAP analysis revealed the most influential physical traits in zygosity prediction, further substantiating the efficacy of XGBoost with SMOTE oversampling as an optimal framework for twin zygosity estimation through self-reported questionnaires. The study's findings have implications for reducing costs associated with twin research and provide a scalable alternative to genetic testing. Future studies will involve external validation and comparison of predicted results with genetic test outcomes, contingent upon sufficient funding.
label: llm

text:
In the field of epilepsy diagnosis, accurate recognition of seizures from electroencephalogram (EEG) data is crucial for timely intervention and informed decision-making. However, traditional centralized machine learning approaches pose significant privacy risks by necessitating the sharing of sensitive patient information across institutions. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework that leverages federated and explainable machine learning to support epileptic seizure recognition while preserving data confidentiality.

Our study employs a publicly available EEG-derived dataset comprising five original classes, which are reformulated into a binary classification task distinguishing between seizure and non-seizure events. Data partitioning at the patient level is implemented to prevent information leakage, with separate training and testing sets established. A simulated federated learning environment is developed, incorporating multiple clients with both independent and non-independent data distributions, and executed over fifty communication rounds using the Federated Averaging strategy.

We evaluate the performance of various machine learning models, including Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and K Nearest Neighbors, using standardized hyperparameter configurations. Model efficacy is assessed through accuracy, area under the ROC curve, confusion matrices, and learning curves. To provide transparency into model decision-making, explainability techniques such as SHAP and LIME are applied.

The results demonstrate that federated learning can achieve competitive performance while safeguarding data privacy, and that explainable methods offer valuable insights into seizure classification decisions. This study serves as a proof-of-concept evaluation using an EEG-derived dataset, highlighting the methodological feasibility of this approach rather than its clinical deployment potential.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of digital documents has led to an increased reliance on tables as a fundamental format for representing structured data across various domains, including healthcare, education, and finance. Table recognition is a critical task aimed at automatically converting table content from images into editable and machine-readable formats, thereby enhancing processing efficiency and accuracy. Despite significant advancements in convolutional neural network (CNN)-based and Transformer-based approaches, existing methods continue to face challenges in handling complex table layouts, diverse formats, and high computational costs. To address these limitations, we introduce the Spatial Pyramid Pooling-enhanced Multi-modal Linear Transformer (SPPLTR), a novel table-recognition model that integrates spatial pyramid pooling with a causal linear-Transformer framework. SPPLTR effectively captures both structural and textual information in table images through a spatial pyramid context fusion module and a causal linear Transformer, thereby improving the understanding of table structures. Furthermore, the model incorporates a multi-head causal linear attention mechanism with automatic mixed precision support, enabling more efficient and stable inference. Comprehensive experiments conducted on the PubTabNet dataset demonstrate that SPPLTR achieves state-of-the-art performance, surpassing most existing models across various evaluation metrics. The proposed model presents a promising solution for table recognition tasks and paves the way for further advancements in structured document understanding.
label: llm

text: Wind turbine systems operating in cold climates are susceptible to icing events, which can compromise structural integrity if undetected. To address this critical issue, we have developed an intelligent information fusion framework that combines vibration-based structural dynamics with environmental data through a pipeline of automated Operational Modal Analysis (OMA) and temperature compensation. This study evaluates the performance of five classification architectures—Optimized Threshold (OT), Random Forest (RF), XGBoost (XGB), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)—using high-fidelity experimental data from a large-scale climate chamber, processed through rolling-window time-series cross-validation. To ensure the reliability of our framework in safety-critical applications, we prioritize Recall to minimize False Negative errors and incorporate non-parametric statistical validation techniques, including Kruskal-Wallis tests and Dunn’s post-hoc analysis, to quantify model stability. Our results demonstrate a significant performance gap between physics-informed models and advanced ensemble and deep learning methods, with the OT model achieving an exceptional operational reliability of 96.00%. This finding highlights the importance of meticulous feature-level fusion and domain-specific engineering in enhancing system resilience, rather than relying on increased algorithmic complexity. The proposed methodology provides a scalable foundation for multi-sensor fusion and adaptive predictive maintenance in renewable energy infrastructures, offering a promising solution to ensure the safe and reliable operation of wind turbine systems in cold climates.
label: llm

text:
Here's an original abstract for the machine learning study:

The proliferation of deepfake content on social media platforms poses significant challenges to maintaining trust and preventing misinformation. As a result, detecting machine-generated textual content is crucial in mitigating its adverse effects. This research aims to develop an intelligent framework for identifying short texts (e.g., tweets) as either human-generated or bot-created. The proposed framework leverages the DeBERTaV3 model, coupled with the TweepFake and PHEME datasets, to analyze social media posts and determine their origin. In contrast to existing approaches, this study evaluates the efficacy of various deep learning frameworks, including BERT, RoBERTa, SVM-RBF, random forest, SVM, CNN, and LSTM, in detecting fake news. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an accuracy rate of 97.12%, outperforming other models in terms of efficiency and effectiveness. The study's findings highlight the significance of data preprocessing techniques and embedding methods in facilitating accurate tweet classification, ultimately enabling the identification of machine-generated content on social media platforms.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the efficacy of machine learning-based credit-scoring models relative to traditional statistical methods using a real-world dataset comprising 1000 loan applications. The primary objective is to assess whether AI-driven approaches can offer superior predictive accuracy, interpretability, and stability in the face of macroeconomic downturns compared to conventional techniques. Four model variants are developed: logistic regression and decision trees as examples of classical models, and XGBoost gradient-boosting ensemble and multilayer perceptron neural networks as alternatives based on AI. The R programming environment (v4.3.1) is employed to implement a 70:30 train-test split, inner cross-validation, and harmony spatial search for hyperparameter tuning. Results indicate that the XGBoost model achieves optimal performance with a cumulative gain curve of 0.89 and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.89, outperforming the neural network (CGC: 0.87; AUC: 0.87) and logistic regression (CGC: 0.76; AUC: 0.76). SHAP analysis reveals that credit amount, loan duration, and age are key predictors of creditworthiness. Notably, XGBoost exhibits stability in predictions during stress-testing (AUC = 0.83), whereas logistic regression performance declines significantly (AUC = 0.68). The findings support the notion that AI-powered tools possess superior predictive capabilities and interpretability, rendering them viable alternatives to traditional methods in modern financial institutions.
label: llm

text: The accurate assignment of imaging protocols is a crucial aspect of radiology workflows, requiring precise and efficient classification of complex medical images. This study investigates the capabilities of GPT-4 in text-based imaging protocol classification, comparing its performance to that of human experts and established fine-tuned models. We evaluated GPT-4 alongside a fine-tuned BERT model and a fine-tuned LLaMA-3 model on clinical indications corresponding to 11 head MRI protocol categories. Model predictions were benchmarked against expert-validated ground truth labels using F1 scores, with each model's reasoning coherence reviewed by a board-certified radiologist. Notably, GPT-4 achieved an F1 score of 0.83, which while lower than the 0.91 and 0.93 obtained by BERT and LLaMA-3 respectively, was surpassed only by human experts with a score of 0.96. However, GPT-4 consistently produced highly interpretable explanations that demonstrated a nuanced understanding of clinical language and imaging rationale. Furthermore, when prompted by the output of a fine-tuned classifier, specifically LLaMA-3, GPT-4's accuracy improved substantially, suggesting that structured collaboration between general and specialized models can enhance both performance and transparency in decision-support workflows. These findings indicate that GPT-4 has potential as an interpretable tool for supporting clinical decision-making when used appropriately.
label: llm

text:
The molecular underpinnings of clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), an aggressive malignancy characterized by single-nucleotide variants in VHL, PBRM1, and SETD2, have been extensively studied. Nevertheless, the contributions of structural variation (SV) and three-dimensional genome architecture to ccRCC pathogenesis remain poorly understood. To address this knowledge gap, we conducted a comprehensive multi-omics analysis to elucidate the role of SV in driving oncogenic dysregulation during ccRCC progression.

Our investigation revealed novel associations between SV and oncogenic targets, as well as multidimensional reorganization of three-dimensional genome architecture. Notably, we identified an uncharacterized pathogenic enhancer hijacking event that leads to constitutive activation of the proto-oncogene SEMA5B, which was experimentally validated through functional assays. To further translate these findings into clinical practice, we developed a machine learning-based prognostic framework using enhancer hijacking signatures.

The results of our study provide new insights into the complex interplay between SV and three-dimensional chromatin architecture in driving ccRCC oncogenesis. Moreover, the development of a prognostic tool based on enhancer hijacking signatures offers a valuable resource for clinicians seeking to improve patient outcomes through more accurate risk stratification and personalized treatment strategies.
label: llm

text:
The global prevalence of anemia is staggering, affecting over one billion individuals worldwide. This debilitating condition arises from insufficient hemoglobin levels due to iron or vitamin deficiencies, blood loss, or genetic disorders such as sickle cell disease and thalassemia. The diagnosis of anemia poses significant challenges in resource-constrained settings, particularly rural areas, where costly blood tests, limited medical personnel, and inadequate infrastructure hinder timely intervention.

To address these limitations, machine learning models have been explored for their potential to analyze blood count records and images from various bodily sites, including the eye conjunctiva, lip mucosa, fingernails, palms, and blood smears. This study undertakes a comprehensive review of artificial intelligence techniques applied to the diagnosis of three distinct types of anemia: iron deficiency, sickle cell disease, and thalassemia. A comparative analysis is conducted on machine learning, deep learning, optimization algorithms, hybrid approaches, and other methods in terms of accuracy, key features, research gaps, and future prospects.

Furthermore, this investigation examines the correlation between dataset sizes and model accuracy to inform best practices for anemia diagnosis using artificial intelligence. The integration of AI into real-time anemia diagnosis offers a promising solution, providing non-invasive, quick, affordable, and accurate diagnoses with explainable AI techniques that enhance physician trust and usability in remote areas. Although traditional invasive tests remain the gold standard for accurate anemia diagnosis, incorporating AI can significantly reduce the burden on healthcare professionals and facilitate diagnostic confirmation.
label: llm

text: The Iraqi construction industry plays a pivotal role in driving economic growth within the country. However, managing housing projects efficiently remains a significant challenge due to uncertainties surrounding their productivity. This study investigates the application of machine learning techniques for predicting the implementation productivity of housing projects in Iraq based on project design and execution characteristics. Three advanced predictive models were developed and evaluated: artificial neural networks (ANN), extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost), and support vector regression (SVR). A dataset comprising 67 actual housing projects was utilized, featuring seven input variables and one output variable representing implementation productivity. The evaluation results indicate that the XGBoost model demonstrated exceptional predictive accuracy with a coefficient of determination (R2) of 0.998 and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 0.8%. In comparison, the ANN model also exhibited strong performance (R2 = 0.92 and MAPE = 4.1%). Conversely, the SVR model showed relatively lower accuracy with an R2 value of 0.813 and MAPE of 6.6%. Feature importance analysis revealed that built-up area was the most influential factor in productivity, followed by quantities of brick and concrete works. The findings suggest that machine learning techniques, particularly XGBoost and ANN, can serve as effective tools for supporting decision-making during the early stages of housing project planning by predicting executive performance based on project data.
label: llm

text: Advances in machine learning and computational methods have the potential to significantly accelerate materials discovery by enabling accurate predictions of material properties at reduced costs. However, a major bottleneck remains in providing high-quality input data to algorithms and optimizing model parameters. High-entropy materials, a distinct class of valuable materials, hold promise for overcoming limitations in conventional battery materials. By leveraging learning algorithms and quantum computing, researchers can design and fabricate novel materials for batteries and supercapacitors before chemical synthesis occurs. Our study focuses on the intersection of quantum computing and high-entropy material structure, particularly high-entropy MXenes. We examine strategies for encoding and decoding crystal structures, a crucial aspect in developing comprehensive databases for high-entropy materials. Furthermore, we investigate the application of deep learning algorithms to facilitate material discovery prior to synthesis, as well as their potential for identifying suitable high-entropy materials for energy storage applications. Ultimately, our research explores the emerging opportunities presented by quantum computing and artificial intelligence approaches for determining high-entropy material structures in the context of energy-related fields.
label: llm

text: This study proposes an integrated artificial intelligence-driven framework to enhance Management Information Systems (MIS) capabilities for analyzing sustainable supply chains and environmental impact. To address three core tasks, research employs a combination of classical machine learning regression models, including Linear Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, and SVR, alongside a deep learning-based Gated TabTransformer (GTT). Additionally, a Variational Graph Autoencoder (VGAE) is utilized for unsupervised anomaly detection, while a hybrid interpretability model constructed with Explainable Boosting Machines (EBM) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) provides explainable insights. The results demonstrate that Linear Regression and ensemble models exhibit superior performance in terms of accuracy and robustness, outperforming existing methods. Notably, the Gated TabTransformer achieves strong generalization capabilities, while the Variational Graph Autoencoder detects anomalies by learning topological and feature-level deviations. Furthermore, the framework enables interpretable local and global insights through EBM-SHAP, facilitating data-driven decision making with transparency. This comprehensive solution bridges sustainability, artificial intelligence, and MIS, advancing the state-of-the-art in sustainability analytics for supply chains.
label: llm

text:
The increasing adoption of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) in military and civilian applications has created a pressing need for robust security measures to mitigate the risk of routing attacks. These networks, comprised of numerous small, low-cost devices, are inherently vulnerable to various types of assaults when deployed in exposed and unsecured environments. The susceptibility of WSNs to attacks such as wormhole, sinkhole, sybil, and black hole attacks poses significant security threats, including exploitation of sensor node vulnerabilities and wireless network unreliability.

This research aims to develop an effective Intrusion Detection System (IDS) for cluster-based WSNs, where a Cluster Head (CH) is selected within each cluster and a Group Head (GH) oversees the behavior of nodes and networks. A novel Dual Level Node Authentication Model with Node Behaviour Analysis (DLNA-NBA) is proposed, incorporating machine learning techniques to enhance intrusion detection accuracy.

The results demonstrate that DLNA-NBA significantly improves intrusion detection capabilities, achieving 98.9% accuracy in dual-level node authentication and 99.2% accuracy in intrusion detection. The proposed model's improved node verification, optimized feature utilization, and efficient behavioral pattern analysis ensure secure communication and reduced false alarms in WSN environments. Overall, this research contributes to the development of more secure and reliable WSNs by providing a robust IDS that can effectively detect and prevent routing attacks.
label: llm

text:
This study investigates the application of machine learning (ML) models for predicting sports-related injuries in professional football players. A dataset comprising 300 male athletes aged 18-28, monitored over two competitive seasons (2021-2022), was analyzed to identify factors contributing to injury risk. Injuries were defined as musculoskeletal conditions causing at least one missed training session or match, confirmed via ICD-10 diagnoses. Daily data on training workload, recovery, wellness, heart-rate variability, cumulative minutes played, and injury history were collected and preprocessed using normalization, one-hot encoding, and feature selection techniques.

The study employed 10-fold cross-validation to train logistic regression, decision tree, and random forest models, evaluating their accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC). Results indicated that random forests outperformed other models, achieving an average accuracy of 85.6% ± 2.1%, precision of 82.1% ± 1.9%, recall of 80.3% ± 2.4%, F1-score of 81.2% ± 2.2%, and AUC of 90.5% ± 1.6%. Explainable AI techniques, including SHAP and LIME, were applied to identify the strongest predictors of injury risk, highlighting prior injury, training intensity, and recovery time as key factors.

The findings demonstrate that ensemble ML methods can provide robust, interpretable, and actionable insights for injury prevention in professional football players. These results support data-driven strategies to optimize training and reduce injury incidence. Future research should aim to validate the predictive models across multiple sports and integrate additional physiological and genetic factors to enhance their accuracy and generalizability.
label: llm

text:
The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into precision medicine has revolutionized healthcare by enabling more personalized treatment approaches. However, the economic viability of this paradigm shift remains a subject of debate. This study aimed to investigate the cost-effectiveness of AI-powered precision medicine through a comprehensive review and analysis of existing economic evaluations.

A systematic search of five major databases was conducted to identify relevant studies, with data extraction and risk-of-bias assessment performed using the ECOBIAS checklist. The findings suggest that AI-PM is cost-saving or cost-effective in approximately 89% of base-case analyses, with incremental cost-effectiveness ratios ranging from dominant to $129,174 per quality-adjusted life-year (QALY). Notably, the interquartile ranges of incremental costs and QALY gains indicate modest health benefits at minimal additional expenses.

The results also highlight significant value heterogeneity, with modeling choices and system-level factors emerging as key sources of uncertainty in estimated net monetary benefits. Furthermore, this study reveals that AI-PM adoption is hindered by low adaptability and underreported key value factors, underscoring the need for further research to address these knowledge gaps. Overall, this study provides valuable insights into the economic feasibility of AI-powered precision medicine, with implications for healthcare policymakers and stakeholders seeking to optimize resource allocation.
label: llm

text:
Here is a rewritten abstract for the machine learning study:

This study addresses the pressing need to improve postoperative sepsis prediction in patients undergoing upper urinary tract stone surgery. Sepsis remains a leading cause of perioperative mortality, necessitating the development of accurate predictive models. This research aims to integrate laboratory parameters using machine learning (ML) and Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) algorithm to create a simple yet effective model for postoperative sepsis prediction. A comprehensive dataset comprising 7,464 patients was analyzed, including 155 pre- and postoperative features. Key variables were selected through Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO) regression and correlation analysis, which facilitated the development of eight machine learning algorithms. The model's performance was rigorously evaluated using Receiver Operating Characteristic curve, learning curve, calibration plot, and decision curve. The SHAP algorithm was employed to analyze variable importance, providing transparent insights into the key drivers of urosepsis. Notably, 116 out of 155 variables differed significantly between the urosepsis and non-urosepsis groups, with eight predictive variables identified through LASSO regression: postoperative IL-6, SAA, PCT/ALB, NLPR, PT, ALB, HCT, and neutrophil. The Light Gradient Boosting Machine achieved superior performance, with AUCs of 1.0, 0.90, and 0.88 for the training, validation, and test cohorts, respectively. The model's robustness was confirmed by a good fit, strong calibration, and positive clinical utility. Postoperative PCT/ALB, neutrophil, IL-6, ALB, and PT were identified as key predictors through SHAP analysis. A publicly accessible web application was developed to facilitate both patients and physicians in early risk stratification and personalized intervention. This study demonstrates the successful development and validation of a robust, interpretable model based on postoperative laboratory biomarkers, offering a practical tool for sepsis prediction and prevention in clinical practice.
label: llm

text:
In recent years, the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in higher education has become increasingly mandatory, leading to a significant increase in psychological and emotional strain among students. Traditional models of technology adoption often fail to account for the challenges posed by compulsory digital environments, where design flaws can result in cognitive overload and student resistance. This study aims to address this gap by developing and validating a student-centered framework that examines the impact of AI system design on negative technological experiences. Specifically, we investigate how system reliability, task routinization, and information richness influence students' perceptions of AI systems.

A quantitative research design was employed, with data collected from 229 undergraduate students across Malaysian universities. Exploratory factor analysis, correlation analysis, and multiple regression were used to examine relationships among key constructs and validate the hypothesized model. Our findings indicate that system reliability and information richness are significant predictors of reduced negative technological experiences, whereas task routinization has no such effect. Notably, we found that negative technological experience serves as a mediator, amplifying techno-resistance and decreasing academic performance.

The results of this study have important implications for the design of AI educational technologies in compulsory settings. By reconceptualizing techno-resistance as an adaptive response to cognitive and emotional strain, our research advances theoretical understanding by integrating digital dissonance with sociotechnical and cognitive load perspectives. The findings highlight the need for more student-aligned AI systems that prioritize system reliability, information richness, and user-centered design principles to mitigate negative technological experiences and promote improved educational outcomes.
label: llm

text:
Here is the rewritten abstract:

The advent of quantum computing has sparked interest in its potential to simulate complex quantum systems more efficiently than classical computers. However, recent studies have demonstrated that machine learning algorithms can effectively predict various properties of quantum systems using only classical data. Despite theoretical proposals for tasks where quantum advantages are evident, these examples often rely on cryptographic functions and do not reflect real-world laboratory scenarios. This paper addresses this gap by establishing a provable quantum advantage in learning quantum observables from measured-out classical data. We investigate two types of observables: first, we demonstrate a learning advantage for linear combinations of Pauli strings; subsequently, we extend our findings to a broader class of unitarily parametrized observables. For each case, we identify the boundaries between tasks that can be efficiently learned classically and those requiring quantum resources. Our classical hardness results rely on the assumption that BQP-hard processes cannot be simulated by polynomial-size classical circuits, and we also provide an efficient quantum learning algorithm. These findings clarify when quantum computing offers practical benefits for learning problems in quantum many-body physics and suggest potential directions for future research into quantum learning improvements.
label: llm

text:
Deep learning has witnessed significant advancements with the emergence of generative adversarial networks (GANs) as a pivotal framework for generating synthetic data. GANs have been widely adopted across various applications, including high-resolution image synthesis, speech generation, medical data reconstruction, educational data simulation, and style transfer. Despite their success in improving quality and diversity of generated data, challenges persist in convergence instability, mode collapse, sensitivity to hyperparameters, and evaluating the quality of synthetic data.

This study aims to provide an in-depth analysis of 45 distinct GAN architectures by categorizing them based on learning paradigm, application domain, network design, associated datasets, and open challenges. By examining the evolution of these architectures, this research seeks to outline prospective avenues for future research, including the integration of GANs with reinforcement learning, attention-based architectures, and large language models (LLMs). The framework presented in this study offers a comprehensive perspective on current trends and opportunities in the GAN landscape, providing researchers and practitioners with a coherent understanding of the field's trajectory.
label: llm

text:
Urban recommender systems are increasingly pivotal in shaping equitable, efficient, and citizen-centric urban services as cities integrate pervasive sensing technologies, data platforms, and artificial intelligence. This comprehensive review synthesizes a vast body of peer-reviewed research across six key domains: mobility, healthcare, energy, tourism, retail, and e-governance. Our analysis reveals that collaborative filtering, content-based methods, hybrid designs, deep learning, and context-aware approaches are being increasingly linked to urban decision-making needs through the development of novel taxonomies.

The review highlights the uneven distribution of advanced techniques such as deep and graph-based models across governance and energy domains, with most empirical studies concentrated in mobility and tourism. We examine how heterogeneous data sources, including IoT streams, geospatial signals, environmental indicators, and demographic attributes, are being integrated to support personalized recommendations under constraints such as latency, reliability, and privacy.

Notably, our review identifies recent advances that address sparsity and cold start through the use of graph neural models, sequence modeling, and transfer learning. Additionally, we cover operational enablers such as edge inference and streaming architectures for real-time recommendation. The survey also assesses risk and governance dimensions, including privacy preservation, fairness, exposure balance across neighborhoods, explainability, and mechanisms for audit and oversight.

Our analysis reveals opportunities in pollution management, citizen education, and participatory platforms that broaden civic engagement. Furthermore, we outline how emerging technologies such as hybrid physical-virtual interactions, digital twins, immersive interfaces, generative models, and quantum algorithms may reshape personalization and oversight in city-scale settings. Finally, we emphasize the need for field evaluations and standardized benchmarks to jointly measure accuracy, latency, robustness to distribution shift, and equity.
label: llm

text:
This study investigates the cellular impacts of genetic variants associated with Alzheimer's disease (AD). Genome-wide association studies have implicated immune and metabolic pathways in AD, but their specific roles remain unclear. To address this knowledge gap, we employed bidirectional two-sample Mendelian randomization to identify single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP)-mediated immunometabolic signatures in AD patients. Our analysis revealed both positively and negatively correlated immune cell types and metabolic pathways, which were further refined through integrated single-cell omics analysis of patient brains and peripheral blood samples.

The results showed distinct astrocyte subpopulations in patient brains with varying associations with AD, as well as predominantly AD-negative metabolic signatures in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs). Leveraging these findings, we developed a blood transcriptome-based AD prediction model using machine learning approaches. The optimized model is applicable to both European and Asian populations, enabling pre-symptomatic detection of familial AD and effective discrimination from other neurodegenerative disorders. Our study provides novel evidence for the critical role of immunometabolism in AD and offers a practical predictive tool suitable for large-scale population screening.
label: llm

text:
Machine Learning Models for Predicting Surgical Correction Dosage in Horizontal Non-Paretic Strabismus

The variability of surgical outcomes in strabismus surgery remains a pressing concern, underscoring the need for more accurate and reliable methods to predict dosage. This study investigates the application of machine learning models to forecast dosages based on preoperative features, with a focus on explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques to determine feature importance. A retrospective analysis of 767 patients who underwent strabismus surgery between 2003 and 2022 at the University Clinic of Ulm was conducted, resulting in a streamlined dataset for model development. Using a multilayer perceptron implemented with the torch neural network library, we evaluated the performance of various models, including the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno algorithm. The results indicate that this algorithm achieved the highest number of predictions within an acceptable range, with root mean square errors (RMSE) of 0.54 mm and 0.71 mm for labels L1 and L2, respectively. Notably, feature permutation and integrated gradients analysis revealed the preoperative angle of deviation as the most critical factor in dosage prediction, followed by near angle of deviation, simultaneous cover test, Hirschberg test angle, and refraction of own glasses used. The findings suggest that machine learning models, particularly those incorporating XAI techniques, hold promise for improving surgical outcome predictions in strabismus surgery. Future work will focus on expanding the dataset to enhance prediction accuracy.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of fake news on social media poses a significant threat to civic trust and public safety, with emotionally charged content exacerbating its spread through user interactions. To address this challenge, we aim to develop an effective framework for detecting anomalous propagation subgraphs indicative of coordinated misinformation campaigns. This study proposes the Multi-Graph Neural Network Attention-based Propagation Learning for Emotion-Aware Anomaly Detection (MAPLE) framework, which integrates sentiment features from users' historical posts with network structures through a novel attention-based fusion mechanism. By combining multiple Graph Neural Networks (GCN, GAT, and GraphSAGE), MAPLE leverages the strengths of each model to improve anomaly detection performance. Theoretical analyses demonstrate that MAPLE preserves mutual information, reduces variance, amplifies anomaly margins, and maximizes entropy. Empirical evaluations on the PolitiFact and GossipCop datasets reveal that MAPLE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving F1-score improvements of 43% and 2.17%, respectively, while maintaining robustness across datasets. The proposed framework provides a unified multi-Graph Neural Network fusion approach with theoretical underpinnings for subgraph anomaly detection in the context of emotionally charged content.
label: llm

text:
High-Dimensional Feature Selection via Dynamic Binary Optimization

The proliferation of high-dimensional datasets in machine learning has underscored the need for efficient feature selection techniques to mitigate computational complexity and enhance classifier generalization. Traditional binary metaheuristics often rely on static control parameters, which can lead to suboptimal performance due to premature convergence and poor adaptability. To address these limitations, this study introduces the Dynamic Binary Swordfish Movement Optimization Algorithm (DBSMOA), a novel nature-inspired binary optimizer that leverages the foraging dynamics of swordfish to navigate complex search spaces.

The proposed algorithm extends the original Swordfish Movement Optimization Algorithm by incorporating dynamic behavioral adaptation mechanisms, enabling a self-adjusting balance between exploration and exploitation throughout the optimization process. DBSMOA employs a sigmoid-based probabilistic binary mapping and dynamically reassesses agent roles through a performance-driven elitist strategy to maintain diversity and stability during search.

Comprehensive evaluations on 52 benchmark datasets demonstrate that DBSMOA achieves superior performance compared to twelve state-of-the-art binary optimizers, including Binary Particle Swarm Optimization (bPSO), Binary Genetic Algorithm (bGA), and Binary Grey Wolf Optimizer (bGWO). The results indicate that DBSMOA delivers competitive accuracy, compact feature subsets, and strong computational efficiency, highlighting its robustness, scalability, and suitability for high-dimensional feature selection and other binary optimization tasks.
label: llm

text:
This study addresses the pressing need for non-invasive and accessible screening methods for high-grade cervical intraepithelial neoplasia (CIN2/3), a precursor to cervical cancer, particularly in resource-limited settings. Current screening approaches, such as HPV testing and colposcopy, are limited by their invasiveness and accessibility issues. To mitigate these challenges, we developed a machine learning-based model that leverages routine blood biomarkers to assess the risk of high-grade CIN.

Our approach involved collecting data from 248 patients, comprising 128 with high-grade CIN (CIN2/3) and 120 with low-grade CIN (CIN1), who underwent comprehensive clinical evaluations. A total of 29 clinical characteristics and blood test measurements were considered for model development. We employed four feature selection algorithms to identify key predictors and trained 11 machine learning models using a dataset split into training (70%) and testing (30%) cohorts.

Model performance was evaluated using multiple metrics, including learning curves, receiver operating characteristic curves (ROC), area under the curve (AUC), Brier score, calibration curves, Precision-Recall (PR) curves, and Decision Curve Analysis (DCA). We also developed a web-based calculator for clinical deployment. To interpret model results, we utilized the SHapley Additive exPlanation (SHAP) approach to assess feature importance.

Our findings indicate that the Support Vector Machine (SVM) algorithm achieved the best predictive performance, with an AUC of 0.75 (95% CI: 0.69–0.80) and a Brier score of 0.21 (95% CI: 0.17–0.28). The identified key features included creatinine, red blood cell count, neutrophil ratio, direct bilirubin, and monocyte count. We also developed a web-based tool for real-time risk stratification, providing a potential solution for clinical utility in resource-limited settings.
label: llm

text:
The design of functional materials necessitates the concurrent optimization of multiple interdependent properties to achieve optimal performance in various applications. In the context of boron-doped graphene (BDG), both the band gap and work function play critical roles, yet existing machine learning approaches often focus on predicting a single property using hand-crafted features, which hinders their applicability across different scenarios.

To address this limitation, we propose an adaptive edge-aware graph convolutional neural network with multi-task learning (AEGCNN-MTL) for simultaneous prediction of multiple material properties. Our approach leverages the strengths of graph neural networks and multi-task learning to capture complex relationships between interdependent properties. We evaluate AEGCNN-MTL on a dataset of 2613 DFT-computed BDG structures, demonstrating exceptional accuracy in predicting band gap (R² = 0.9905) and work function (R² = 0.9778). Notably, our framework outperforms single-task GNN baselines under identical training budgets, showcasing its potential for efficient multi-property prediction.

Furthermore, we transfer AEGCNN-MTL to the QM9 benchmark, where it exhibits competitive performance across 12 diverse quantum chemical properties, underscoring its strong generalization capability. These results highlight the promise of AEGCNN-MTL as a scalable and accurate tool for high-throughput material screening and data-driven discovery of multifunctional materials.
label: llm

text:
This study introduces an innovative method for generating a fingerprint for crystalline materials that reconciles efficiency in machine processing with human interpretability. The proposed approach comprises two key components: a structural representation capturing the crystal structure and a compositional component, referred to as Pettifor embedding, which characterizes the chemical composition.

The Pettifor embedding is constructed within a non-orthogonal space where each axis corresponds to a specific chemical element, with angles between axes quantifying similarity. The chemical composition is then defined by a point on the unit sphere in this space. Our results demonstrate that Pettifor embeddings outperform conventional elemental embeddings in compositional machine learning models.

Utilizing Pettifor embeddings as a distance metric and dimension reduction techniques, we develop a two-dimensional global map of thermodynamically stable crystalline compounds. Notably, these maps effectively separate material classes based on fundamental physical properties, despite their relatively simple construction. Our findings highlight the potential of Pettifor embeddings to facilitate a deeper understanding of structure-property relationships in crystalline materials and inform the development of more accurate machine learning models for materials science applications.
label: llm

text: Ecosystems play a critical role in mitigating the impacts of climate change by absorbing significant portions of anthropogenic CO2 emissions. However, understanding ecosystem responses to climate variability requires accurate and consistent monitoring of carbon, water, and energy fluxes across diverse landscapes. Existing datasets often lack sufficient spatial resolution and consistency, particularly in fragmented regions such as UK agricultural areas. To address this gap, we present the Unified FLUXes (UFLUX) ensemble, a novel dataset of gross primary productivity, evapotranspiration, and sensible heat fluxes derived from eddy covariance data, satellite observations, and machine learning algorithms. UFLUX comprises approximately 60 ensemble members spanning multiple spatial and temporal scales, including global monthly averages at 0.25° resolution, European daily and biannual datasets at 0.25° and 100 m resolutions respectively, and UK daily datasets at 100 m resolution. Validation against eddy covariance measurements indicates that UFLUX captures over 80% of flux variability with low mean absolute errors, accurately reproducing climate responses and interannual patterns consistent with existing research. While uncertainties in net carbon flux remain, the UFLUX dataset holds significant promise for informing cross-scale climate policymaking and land management strategies aimed at achieving a carbon-neutral future.
label: llm

text: The proliferation of multiplayer gaming has given rise to an escalating cat-and-mouse game between anticheat developers and cheaters, necessitating the development of robust anti-cheat mechanisms to ensure fair play. In response, anti-cheat systems have evolved significantly over the past few decades, incorporating advanced cryptographic techniques to counter various forms of cheating. However, conventional methods such as signature-based detection and memory scanning are increasingly ineffective against novel and adaptive threats. This study examines the limitations of these traditional approaches and explores modern AI-driven solutions, including computer vision-based aimbot detection and behavioral analysis using deep learning models. These cutting-edge techniques offer proactive and adaptive capabilities that can detect zero-day threats more effectively than conventional methods. Through a comparative analysis, this research identifies a critical gap in existing literature and proposes a novel hybrid security framework model that integrates the strengths of traditional anti-cheat methods with the dynamic detection power of AI-driven solutions. The proposed architecture leverages cryptographic technologies such as Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) for process isolation and Hierarchical Identity-Based Encryption (HIBE) for secure authentication, creating a resilient multilayered defense against cheating. Ultimately, this study provides a technical reference and architectural blueprint for designing the next generation of secure, cheat-resistant online gaming systems, with future research directions focused on integrating post-quantum cryptography and developing lightweight frameworks for mobile and VR platforms.
label: llm

text: The optimization of sintered neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) magnets is a complex task, hindered by intricate structure-property relationships and limited data availability. To address this challenge, we have compiled the first comprehensive database for this system, encompassing 1994 industrial and academic samples. Our research aims to investigate the efficacy of active learning (AL) strategies in conjunction with classical and quantum-enhanced regressors. A thorough analysis of our curated dataset reveals distinct design heuristics employed by industrial and academic researchers, underscoring the importance of domain-aware considerations in materials science applications. Furthermore, we propose a novel methodological framework for integrating quantum kernel regression into an AL framework, utilizing bootstrapped ensembles to quantify uncertainty. Our experimental results demonstrate that the effectiveness of AL is contingent upon the chosen regressor model. Specifically, significant acceleration is observed when using Random Forest and Support Vector Regression models, whereas XGBoost exhibits diminished performance, and in some cases, AL proves detrimental compared to random sampling. These findings have far-reaching implications for the strategic application of machine learning in materials discovery, highlighting the need for careful model selection and domain-aware analysis in this field.
label: llm

text: This study explores the application of machine learning techniques for modeling and predicting Energetic Intelligence, a construct that encompasses emotional, spiritual, and physical dimensions of human intelligence. Building on recent validation efforts, our research leverages data-driven approaches to investigate the feasibility of using machine learning algorithms in organizational contexts. We collected survey responses from individuals and employed a range of supervised learning methods to develop predictive models. Model performance was assessed through standard metrics, with optimized models achieving an R2 value of 0.73. Notably, variables such as Flow, Flourishing, and Emotional Vitality emerged as key predictors in model training, underscoring their relevance for Energetic Intelligence assessment. These findings suggest that machine learning can provide valuable insights into human potential and well-being when integrated with psychological theory. The study's outcomes have practical implications for the development of data-driven tools to support the assessment and cultivation of Energetic Intelligence in applied settings, highlighting the potential benefits of interdisciplinary collaboration between AI research and psychology.
label: llm

text: The increasing reliance of cyber-physical systems on integrated circuits (ICs) has raised concerns about the vulnerability of these systems to malicious hardware attacks. Hardware Trojans, a type of stealthy threat, are malicious implants in circuitry that can remain dormant until triggered, causing functional disruptions or sensitive information leakage. Traditional IC validation methods often fail to detect these subtle anomalies due to their ability to mimic normal circuit behavior. To address this challenge, we propose a novel approach for detecting hardware Trojans through side-channel power analysis. Our method combines time-domain features with frequency-domain characteristics of power traces using a dual-domain feature extraction strategy. We developed an artificial intelligence (AI) based robust Trojan detection framework that integrates traditional machine learning models, including random forest, gradient boosting, naïve bayes, and deep learning models such as deep neural network, long short-term memory, and graph neural network. A stacked ensemble classifier is employed to leverage the strengths of these baseline models. Our results demonstrate that this ensemble-based detection method outperforms individual baseline models on the advanced encryption standard (AES)-Trojan benchmark, achieving a macro-averaged area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.987 without relying on a trusted reference IC for comparison. This approach enables direct anomaly detection from observable characteristics of untrusted chips, such as side-channel emissions, making it an attractive solution for ensuring the security and integrity of cyber-physical systems.
label: llm

text:
Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a pervasive global health concern, necessitating the development of efficient and accurate prediction methods to inform timely interventions. Traditional diagnostic approaches often suffer from limitations such as prolonged processing times, substantial costs, and susceptibility to human error, underscoring the potential benefits of machine learning (ML) for enhanced risk assessment. However, ML-based CVD prediction is hindered by challenges including outliers, irrelevant features, class imbalance, and limited interpretability, which necessitate a comprehensive framework that addresses these issues.

To this end, we propose an integrated approach that leverages advanced data preprocessing techniques to detect and correct anomalies, feature selection methods to identify the most informative predictors, and ensemble learning strategies to mitigate class imbalance. Specifically, we employ Z-score-based outlier detection and XGBoost-driven feature selection to optimize model performance. Furthermore, we develop a suite of eight ML models, including Logistic Regression, KNN, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, and Gaussian Naive Bayes, which are rigorously evaluated using a range of metrics.

The results demonstrate that a voting ensemble combining the top-performing models (Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and AdaBoost) achieves exceptional accuracy (98.63%) and AUC-ROC (99.13%) on the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset, significantly outperforming individual models. Importantly, SHAP and LIME analyses provide actionable insights into model predictions, facilitating informed clinical decision-making and patient risk stratification. This work contributes a robust, interpretable, and high-performing ML framework for early CVD detection, with potential applications in clinical practice.
label: llm

text:
Predictive Modeling for Timely Identification of Febrile Patients Requiring Hospitalization in Emergency Departments

The timely identification of febrile patients necessitating hospitalization remains a pressing concern within Emergency Departments (EDs). Delayed intervention can lead to increased mortality, underscoring the need for effective predictive models. This study presents an innovative approach utilizing pre-trained language models to forecast ED disposition decisions based on triage information from 25,405 febrile patient visits. A predictive model was developed and validated using vital signs and self-reported symptoms to identify patients requiring hospitalization or readmission within a 72-hour window. The proposed methodology involves transforming numerical vital signs into narrative text, enabling the integration of clinical narratives through a novel architecture. The results demonstrate superior performance compared to traditional machine learning methods and biomedical language models, with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) values reaching 0.9226, area under the precision-recall curve (AUPRC) at 0.8767, and macro F₁-score of 0.8446. Notably, demographic patterns emerged, indicating a higher likelihood of hospitalization among elderly patients, males, and those arriving by ambulance. This predictive model addresses critical ED challenges by facilitating early patient identification, reducing inappropriate discharges, and optimizing resource allocation, ultimately enhancing patient outcomes and ED efficiency.
label: llm

text: This study investigates the interplay between genetic predisposition, lifestyle, and all-cause mortality in the context of obesity. Despite the well-documented influence of genetics on body weight, the complex relationships among genetic susceptibility to obesity, lifestyle choices, and increased risk of mortality remain poorly understood. To address this knowledge gap, we developed and validated a machine learning model that integrates genetic data from two large cohorts: 482,700 UK Biobank participants and 8,607 Nanfang Hospital participants. The model generates an obesity-related polygenic risk score (OPRS), which enables the evaluation of associations between genetic risk of obesity, lifestyle factors, and all-cause mortality. Our results indicate that a high OPRS is significantly correlated with increased all-cause mortality, particularly among individuals with normal weight or overweight status. Notably, adherence to four healthy lifestyle factors among individuals with high genetic risk of obesity was found to reduce the risk of all-cause mortality by 59% compared to those without such lifestyle modifications. These findings highlight the critical role of genetics in determining obesity-related health outcomes and underscore the importance of a healthy lifestyle in mitigating the adverse effects of genetic predisposition on mortality risk.
label: llm

text: In post-mastectomy reconstruction, precise estimation of breast volume and weight is crucial for optimal outcomes. However, existing methods often involve significant costs or complexity. To address these limitations, we developed a machine learning framework that utilizes demographic and anthropometric data to predict breast volume and weight. Our study involved collecting data from 199 patients between 2021 and 2023, which underwent rigorous pre-processing, feature selection, model training, and performance evaluation. We employed three distinct feature selection techniques: domain expert knowledge, Spearman's rank correlation, and the Boruta algorithm. Each feature set was used to train a range of models, including linear regression, random forest regression, and support vector regression. Model performance was assessed using metrics such as R2 values and Pearson's correlation coefficient. Notably, significant correlations were observed between breast volume or weight and patient characteristics like BMI, breast cup size, ptosis severity, and anthropometric measurements. The optimal model, combining domain-expert and statistically selected features via linear regression, achieved R2 values of 81.8% for breast volume and 72% for breast weight. These findings demonstrate that integrating machine learning with demographic and anthropometric data yields a highly accurate, interpretable, and accessible method for preoperative breast assessment. In contrast to conventional imaging or mathematical models, this approach eliminates costs related to equipment, relies on routinely collected clinical data, reduces the need for specialized training, and enables seamless integration into existing clinical workflows. By overcoming traditional methods' limitations, our proposed model provides a practical, efficient, and cost-effective solution for clinical practice.
label: llm

text:
The phenomenon of fracture has far-reaching implications across various disciplines, encompassing infrastructure resilience, aerospace durability, reservoir productivity, and seismic event analysis. To address the need for accurate material failure prediction, this study presents a diverse dataset of simulated fracture evolution and material degradation generated using two numerical solvers: the phase-field method and the combined finite-discrete element method (FDEM). These solvers exhibit distinct formulations, physical fidelity levels, and computational efficiency characteristics.

The dataset comprises five materials: PBX, anisotropic shale, tungsten, aluminum, and steel. For each material, extensive phase-field simulations were conducted, totaling 400,000 cases: 200,000 under uniaxial tension and 200,000 under biaxial tension. In contrast, the computationally intensive FDEM simulations consisted of 90,000 cases, evenly distributed among PBX, shale, and tungsten under uniaxial loading conditions. All simulations initiated with randomly generated initial fracture patterns, capturing temporal data on fracture propagation dynamics.

This comprehensive dataset is designed to facilitate the development of foundational or surrogate machine learning approaches for predicting material failure. By providing a robust foundation for future research, this study aims to advance innovation in areas where accurate material failure prediction is critical. While no specific machine learning models are introduced here, the presented dataset serves as a crucial stepping stone for advancing predictive capabilities in these domains.
label: llm

text:
Here is a rewritten abstract based on the source text:

Developmental dysgraphia is a complex neurological disorder that significantly impairs children's writing abilities, making accurate diagnosis particularly challenging due to its heterogeneous symptoms and frequent co-occurrence with other disorders. Recent research has explored machine learning approaches for dysgraphia diagnosis using either offline or online handwriting data, but these studies have typically analyzed the two modalities separately, neglecting the potential benefits of integrating them. To address this limitation, we propose a novel multimodal machine learning approach that combines both online and offline handwriting data to improve diagnostic accuracy. A unique dataset was created by transforming an existing online handwriting dataset into its offline counterpart, allowing for a comprehensive analysis of word types, including simple words, pseudowords, and difficult words. We employed support vector machines (SVM) and extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) classifiers on both online and offline features, as well as multimodal feature fusion and soft-voted ensemble methods. Additionally, we introduced an innovative ensemble with conditional feature fusion, which selectively integrates feature-level fusion when confidence scores fall below a certain threshold. Our proposed approach achieved an impressive accuracy of 88.8%, outperforming single-modality SVMs by 12-14 percentage points and existing methods by 8-9 percentage points. This methodology offers significant contributions to the preliminary screening of handwriting difficulties through multimodal analysis of word/pseudoword data, providing a promising initial assessment tool that supports dysgraphia diagnosis. The findings highlight the potential of multimodal learning in enhancing dysgraphia assessment, paving the way for more accessible and effective diagnostic tools.
label: llm

text:
The development of effective recommender systems has been hindered by their reliance on a single type of user-item interaction, such as purchases or ratings.
